1: #!/bin/bash
2: 
3: # tmux URL handler script - opens URLs from tmux pane content
4: # Based on: https://dev.to/tomoviktor/best-way-to-open-urls-in-your-terminal-via-tmux-595b
5: # Smart capture: tries visible pane first, falls back to full scrollback if no URLs found
6: 
7: set -e
8: 
9: name="tmux-url-handler"
10: buffer_file_name="tmux-url-buffer-$$"
11: temp_file="/tmp/$buffer_file_name"
12: 
13: # Cleanup function
14: cleanup() {
15:     rm -f "$temp_file"
16: }
17: trap cleanup EXIT
18: 
19: # Function to capture URLs from pane content
20: # Args: $1 = capture mode ("visible" or "full")
21: capture_urls() {
22:     local capture_mode="$1"
23: 
24:     if [ "$capture_mode" = "full" ]; then
25:         tmux capture-pane -J -S - -p >"$temp_file"
26:     else
27:         tmux capture-pane -J -p >"$temp_file"
28:     fi
29: 
30:     # Extract scheme-qualified URLs and bare domains with common TLDs
31:     # Bare domains (e.g. google.com, bbc.co.uk) are normalized to https:// when opened
32:     grep -Eoi "(https?://[a-zA-Z0-9./?=_:%+#&~@!*(),;_%-]+|[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9.-]*\.(com|org|net|io|dev|co\.uk|gov\.uk|edu|info|me|app)\b[/a-zA-Z0-9./?=_:%+#&~@!*(),;_%-]*)" "$temp_file" | sort | uniq || true
33: }
34: 
35: # Smart capture: try visible pane first, fall back to full scrollback if no URLs found
36: urls=$(capture_urls "visible")
37: 
38: if [ -z "$urls" ]; then
39:   ...

1: #!/bin/bash
2: 
3: # tmux URL handler script - opens URLs from tmux pane content
4: # Based on: https://dev.to/tomoviktor/best-way-to-open-urls-in-your-terminal-via-tmux-595b
5: # Smart capture: tries visible pane first, falls back to full scrollback if no URLs found
6: 
7: set -e
8: 
9: name="tmux-url-handler"
10: buffer_file_name="tmux-url-buffer-$$"
11: temp_file="/tmp/$buffer_file_name"
12: 
13: # Cleanup function
14: cleanup() {
15:     rm -f "$temp_file"
16: }
17: trap cleanup EXIT
18: 
19: # Function to capture URLs from pane content
20: # Args: $1 = capture mode ("visible" or "full")
21: capture_urls() {
22:     local capture_mode="$1"
23: 
24:     if [ "$capture_mode" = "full" ]; then
25:         tmux capture-pane -J -S - -p >"$temp_file"
26:     else
27:         tmux capture-pane -J -p >"$temp_file"
28:     fi
29: 
30:     # Extract scheme-qualified URLs and bare domains with common TLDs
31:     # Bare domains (e.g. google.com, bbc.co.uk) are normalized to https:// when opened
32:     grep -Eoi "(https?://[a-zA-Z0-9./?=_:%+#&~@!*(),;_%-]+|[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9.-]*\.(com|org|net|io|dev|co\.uk|gov\.uk|edu|info|me|app)\b[/a-zA-Z0-9./?=_:%+#&~@!*(),;_%-]*)" "$temp_file" | sort | uniq || true
33: }
34: 
35: # Smart capture: try visible pane first, fall back to full scrollback if no URLs found
36: urls=$(capture_urls "visible")
37: 
38: if [ -z "$urls" ]; then
39:   ...

  Line 461557: {"type":"message.part.updated","timestamp":"2026-05-04T17:38:21.562Z","session_id":"ses_20c0dd689ffeNecFIlaqCRUIMC","message_id":null,"model":null,"summary":{"sessionID":"ses_20c0dd689ffeNecFIlaqCRUIMC","part":{"id":"prt_df411ce150018mGkZeHkC2Mbfw","messageID":"msg_df41192f60011ImXt6mMNqgoBN","sessionID":"ses_20c0dd689ffeNecFIlaqCRUIMC","type":"text","text":"Fixed the remaining cause: the script was only resizing the focused window, so a terminal could stay maximized if it wasn’t the exact focused window when the callback ran.

Now `focus-layout.sh`:
- Normalizes every normal window in workspace `1`-`4` to the same centered floating size.
- Turns off AeroSpace fullscreen and macOS-native fullscreen per window.
- Skips `MyWallpaper` so it does not get resized into the stack.
- Returns focus to the original window so it stays on top.

Verified live:
- Moved WezTerm into workspace `1`.
- WezTerm bounds: `60,59 | 1391x864`
- Firefox bounds: `60,59 | 1391x864`
- WezTerm state in workspace `1`: `floating|false`
- Restored WezTerm back to `T`, where it remains fullscreen.","time":{"start":1777916300821,"end":1777916301561},"metadata":{"openai":{"itemId":"msg_0ddae1bdbaed9dee0169f8d98ccb5081919ce71dfefdd4dc79","phase":"final_answer"}}},"time":1777916301561}}

<path>/Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/scripts/setup/granted-setup.sh</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: #!/bin/bash
2: # Granted Setup and Management Helper Script
3: # Provides utilities for managing Granted AWS profile colors, icons, and configurations
4: 
5: set -e
6: 
7: # Color codes for output
8: RED='\033[0;31m'
9: GREEN='\033[0;32m'
10: YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
11: BLUE='\033[0;34m'
12: PURPLE='\033[0;35m'
13: CYAN='\033[0;36m'
14: NC='\033[0m' # No Color
15: 
16: # Configuration paths
17: GRANTED_CONFIG_DIR="$HOME/.granted"
18: FIREFOX_PROFILES_FILE="$GRANTED_CONFIG_DIR/firefox-profiles"
19: DOTFILES_CONFIG_DIR="$HOME/dotfiles/.granted"
20: 
21: # Available colors and icons
22: COLORS=(blue turquoise green yellow orange red pink purple)
23: ICONS=(fingerprint briefcase dollar cart circle gift vacation food fruit pet tree chill)
24: 
25: print_usage() {
26:     cat << EOF
27: Granted Setup and Management Helper
28: 
29: Usage: $0 [command] [options]
30: 
31: Commands:
32:     setup                       - Initial setup and configuration
33:     set-profile-color PROFILE COLOR ICON - Set Firefox color/icon for profile
34:     list-profiles              - List configured Firefox profiles
35:     list-colors                - Show available colors and icons
36:     test-config               - Test Granted configuration
37:     backup-config             - Backup current configuration
38:     restore-config            - Restore configuration from dotfiles
39:     help                      - Show this help message
40: 
41: Examples:
42:     $0 setup
43:     $0 set-profile-color production red briefcase
44:     $0 set-profile-color development green tree
45:     $0 list-profiles
46:     $0 test-config
47: 
48: Available Colors: $(printf '%s ' "${COLORS[@]}")
49: Available Icons:  $(printf '%s ' "${ICONS[@]}")
50: EOF
51: }
52: 
53: log_info() {
54:     echo -e "${BLUE}[INFO]${NC} $1"
55: }
56: 
57: log_success() {
58:     echo -e "${GREEN}[SUCCESS]${NC} $1"
59: }
60: 
61: log_warning() {
62:     echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARNING]${NC} $1"
63: }
64: 
65: log_error() {
66:     echo -e "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} $1"
67: }
68: 
69: validate_color() {
70:     local color="$1"
71:     for valid_color in "${COLORS[@]}"; do
72:         if [[ "$color" == "$valid_color" ]]; then
73:             return 0
74:         fi
75:     done
76:     return 1
77: }
78: 
79: validate_icon() {
80:     local icon="$1"
81:     for valid_icon in "${ICONS[@]}"; do
82:         if [[ "$icon" == "$valid_icon" ]]; then
83:             return 0
84:         fi
85:     done
86:     return 1
87: }
88: 
89: setup_granted() {
90:     log_info "Setting up Granted configuration..."
91: 
92:     # Check if Granted is installed
93:     if ! command -v granted &> /dev/null; then
94:         log_error "Granted is not installed. Please install it first with: brew install granted"
95:         exit 1
96:     fi
97: 
98:     # Create config directory
99:     mkdir -p "$GRANTED_CONFIG_DIR"
100: 
101:     # Copy configuration files from dotfiles
102:     if [[ -f "$DOTFILES_CONFIG_DIR/config" ]]; then
103:         log_info "Installing Granted configuration..."
104:         cp "$DOTFILES_CONFIG_DIR/config" "$GRANTED_CONFIG_DIR/config"
105:         log_success "Granted configuration installed"
106:     else
107:         log_warning "Granted config file not found in dotfiles"
108:     fi
109: 
110:     if [[ -f "$DOTFILES_CONFIG_DIR/firefox-profiles" ]]; then
111:         log_info "Installing Firefox profiles configuration..."
112:         cp "$DOTFILES_CONFIG_DIR/firefox-profiles" "$FIREFOX_PROFILES_FILE"
113:         log_success "Firefox profiles configuration installed"
114:     else
115:         log_warning "Firefox profiles config not found in dotfiles"
116:     fi
117: 
118:     # Generate shell completions
119:     if command -v fish &> /dev/null; then
120:         log_info "Generating Fish shell completions..."
121:         mkdir -p "$HOME/.config/fish/completions"
122:         granted completion --shell fish > "$HOME/.config/fish/completions/granted.fish" 2>/dev/null || true
123:         log_success "Fish completions generated"
124:     fi
125: 
126:     if command -v zsh &> /dev/null; then
127:         log_info "Zsh completions will be loaded automatically"
128:     fi
129: 
130:     log_success "Granted setup complete!"
131:     log_info "You can now use 'assume <profile>' to switch AWS profiles"
132:     log_info "Firefox profiles will use colors and icons as configured"
133: }
134: 
135: set_profile_color() {
136:     local profile="$1"
137:     local color="$2"
138:     local icon="$3"
139: 
140:     if [[ -z "$profile" || -z "$color" || -z "$icon" ]]; then
141:         log_error "Usage: set-profile-color PROFILE COLOR ICON"
142:         return 1
143:     fi
144: 
145:     if ! validate_color "$color"; then
146:         log_error "Invalid color: $color"
147:         log_info "Available colors: $(printf '%s ' "${COLORS[@]}")"
148:         return 1
149:     fi
150: 
151:     if ! validate_icon "$icon"; then
152:         log_error "Invalid icon: $icon"
153:         log_info "Available icons: $(printf '%s ' "${ICONS[@]}")"
154:         return 1
155:     fi
156: 
157:     # Ensure Firefox profiles file exists
158:     if [[ ! -f "$FIREFOX_PROFILES_FILE" ]]; then
159:         log_warning "Firefox profiles file not found, creating..."
160:         mkdir -p "$GRANTED_CONFIG_DIR"
161:         touch "$FIREFOX_PROFILES_FILE"
162:     fi
163: 
164:     # Remove existing entry for this profile
165:     grep -v "^$profile=" "$FIREFOX_PROFILES_FILE" > "$FIREFOX_PROFILES_FILE.tmp" 2>/dev/null || true
166: 
167:     # Add new entry
168:     echo "$profile=$color:$icon" >> "$FIREFOX_PROFILES_FILE.tmp"
169: 
170:     # Sort and replace
171:     sort "$FIREFOX_PROFILES_FILE.tmp" > "$FIREFOX_PROFILES_FILE"
172:     rm "$FIREFOX_PROFILES_FILE.tmp"
173: 
174:     log_success "Set $profile to use $color color with $icon icon"
175: }
176: 
177: list_profiles() {
178:     log_info "Configured Firefox profiles:"
179: 
180:     if [[ ! -f "$FIREFOX_PROFILES_FILE" ]]; then
181:         log_warning "No Firefox profiles configuration found"
182:         return 1
183:     fi
184: 
185:     while IFS='=' read -r profile config; do
186:         if [[ -n "$profile" && ! "$profile" =~ ^# ]]; then
187:             IFS=':' read -r color icon <<< "$config"
188:             printf "  %-20s %s%s%s (icon: %s)\n" "$profile" "$color" "$color" "${NC}" "$icon"
189:         fi
190:     done < "$FIREFOX_PROFILES_FILE"
191: }
192: 
193: list_colors() {
194:     log_info "Available Firefox colors and icons:"
195:     echo
196:     echo "Colors:"
197:     for color in "${COLORS[@]}"; do
198:         printf "  %s\n" "$color"
199:     done
200:     echo
201:     echo "Icons:"
202:     for icon in "${ICONS[@]}"; do
203:         printf "  %s\n" "$icon"
204:     done
205: }
206: 
207: test_config() {
208:     log_info "Testing Granted configuration..."
209: 
210:     # Check if Granted is installed
211:     if ! command -v granted &> /dev/null; then
212:         log_error "Granted is not installed"
213:         return 1
214:     fi
215: 
216:     log_success "Granted is installed: $(granted --version)"
217: 
218:     # Check config files

232:

243:         log_success "Fish completions installed"
244:     else
245:         log_warning "Fish completions not found"
246:     fi
247: }
248: 
249: backup_config() {
250:     local backup_dir="$HOME/.granted.backup.$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)"
251:

255:         log_success "Configuration backed up to $backup_dir"
256:     else
257:         log_warning "No configuration to backup"
258:     fi
259: }
260: 
261: restore_config() {

263:     backup_config
264:     setup_granted

    # Handle console flag for Firefox containers
    if test "$argv[1]" = -c
        set -l profile $argv[2]
        if test -n "$profile"
            # First assume the profile using bash with suppressed interactive prompts
            set -l env_vars (printf 'n\n' | bash -c "source /opt/homebrew/bin/assume $profile >/dev/null 2>&1 && env | grep -E '^(AWS_|GRANTED_)'")

18:     if not test -d "$ccb_dir"
19:         echo "ClaudeCodeBrowser not installed. Run scripts/setup.sh or:"
20:         echo "  git clone https://github.com/nanogenomic/ClaudeCodeBrowser.git $ccb_dir"
21:         return 1
22:     end
23: 
24:     set -l cmd $argv[1]
25:     if test -z "$cmd"
26:         set cmd status
27:     end
28: 
29:     switch $cmd
30:         case start
31:             # Check if already running via PID file
32:             if test -f "$pid_file"; and kill -0 (cat "$pid_file") 2>/dev/null
33:                 echo "MCP server already running (PID: "(cat "$pid_file")")"
34:                 return 0
35:             end
36: 
37:             # Kill orphaned processes blocking our ports
38:             for port in 8765 8766
39:                 set -l stale_pid (lsof -ti :$port 2>/dev/null)
40:                 if test -n "$stale_pid"
41:                     echo "Killing orphaned process on port $port (PID: $stale_pid)"
42:                     kill $stale_pid 2>/dev/null
43:                     sleep 0.5
44:                 end
45:             end
46:             rm -f "$pid_file"
47: 
48:             mkdir -p "$log_dir"
49:             echo "Starting MCP server..."
50:             python3 "$ccb_dir/mcp-server/server.py" >"$log_dir/server.log" 2>&1 &
51:             set -l server_pid $last_pid
52:             echo $server_pid >"$pid_file"
53:             sleep 1
54: 
55:             if kill -0 $server_pid 2>/dev/null
56:                 echo "MCP server started (PID: $server_pid)"
57:                 echo "  HTTP: http://127.0.0.1:8765"
58:                 echo "  WebSocket: ws://127.0.0.1:8766"
59:             else
60:                 echo "MCP server failed to start. Check: $log_dir/server.log"
61:                 rm -f "$pid_file"
62:                 return 1
63:             end
64: 
65:         case stop
66:             set -l killed false
67:             if test -f "$pid_file"
68:                 set -l pid (cat "$pid_file")
69:                 if kill -0 $pid 2>/dev/null
70:                     kill $pid
71:                     echo "MCP server stopped (PID: $pid)"
72:                     set killed true
73:                 end
74:                 rm -f "$pid_file"
75:             end
76: 
77:             # Also kill any orphaned processes on our ports
78:             for port in 8765 8766
79:                 set -l stale_pid (lsof -ti :$port 2>/dev/null)
80:                 if test -n "$stale_pid"
81:                     kill $stale_pid 2>/dev/null
82:                     echo "Killed orphaned process on port $port (PID: $stale_pid)"
83:                     set killed true
84:                 end
85:             end
86: 
87:             if test "$killed" = false
88:                 echo "MCP server not running"
89:             end
90: 
91:         case status
92:             echo "ClaudeCodeBrowser Status"
93:             echo "========================"
94:             echo "Install dir: $ccb_dir"
95: 
96:             # Server status
97:             if test -f "$pid_file"; and kill -0 (cat "$pid_file") 2>/dev/null
98:                 echo "MCP server: running (PID: "(cat "$pid_file")")"
99:             else
100:                 echo "MCP server: stopped"
101:             end
102: 
103:             # Port check
104:             if lsof -i :8765 >/dev/null 2>&1
105:                 echo "HTTP port 8765: in use"
106:             else
107:                 echo "HTTP port 8765: available"
108:             end
109: 
110:             if lsof -i :8766 >/dev/null 2>&1
111:                 echo "WS port 8766: in use"
112:             else
113:                 echo "WS port 8766: available"
114:             end
115: 
116:             # CORS check
117:             if test -f "$ccb_dir/mcp-server/server.py"
118:                 if grep -q "Allow-Origin', '\\*'" "$ccb_dir/mcp-server/server.py"
119:                     echo "CORS: UNPATCHED (wildcard - vulnerable)"
120:                 else if grep -q _get_cors_origin "$ccb_dir/mcp-server/server.py"
121:                     echo "CORS: patched (moz-extension:// allowlist)"
122:                 else if grep -q "Allow-Origin', 'null'" "$ccb_dir/mcp-server/server.py"
123:                     echo "CORS: partially patched (null origin - upgrade recommended)"
124:                 else
125:                     echo "CORS: patched (hardened)"
126:                 end
127:             end
128: 
129:             # Extension install reminder
130:             echo ""
131:             echo "Firefox extension: https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/claudecodebrowser/"
132: 
133:         case logs
134:             if test -f "$log_dir/server.log"
135:                 tail -f "$log_dir/server.log"
136:             else
137:                 echo "No log file found at $log_dir/server.log"
138:             end
139: 
140:         case update
141:             echo "Updating ClaudeCodeBrowser..."
142: 
143:             # Check if running
144:             set -l was_running false
145:             if test -f "$pid_file"; and kill -0 (cat "$pid_file") 2>/dev/null
146:                 set was_running true
147:                 ccb stop
148:             end
149: 
150:             # Pull latest
151:             cd "$ccb_dir"
152:             git pull
153:             cd -
154: 
155:             # Re-apply CORS hardening (moz-extension:// allowlist)
156:             if test -f "$ccb_dir/mcp-server/server.py"
157:                 if grep -q "Allow-Origin', '\\*'" "$ccb_dir/mcp-server/server.py"
158:                     # Run the same Python patcher as setup.sh
159:                     python3 -c '
160: import sys, re
161: path = sys.argv[1]
162: with open(path, "r") as f:
163:     content = f.read()
164: helper = """
165:     def _get_cors_origin(self):
166:         origin = self.headers.get("Origin", "")
167:         if origin.startswith("moz-extension://"):
168:             return origin
169:         return ""
170: """
171: content = re.sub(
172:     r"(    def log_message\(self, format, \*args\):\n        logger\.info\(f\"HTTP: \{format % args\}\"\)\n)",
173:     r"\1" + helper,
174:     content
175: )
176: content = content.replace(
177:     "self.send_header('"'"'Access-Control-Allow-Origin'"'"', '"'"'*'"'"')",
178:     "self.send_header('"'"'Access-Control-Allow-Origin'"'"', self._get_cors_origin())"
179: )
180: with open(path, "w") as f:

</content>

metadata.preview:
# ClaudeCodeBrowser management wrapper
# Firefox browser automation for Claude Code via MCP
#
# Usage:
#   ccb start      - Launch MCP server in background
#   ccb stop       - Stop MCP server
#   ccb status     - Show server running state and port availability
#   ccb logs       - Tail server logs
#   ccb update     - git pull + re-apply CORS patch + restart if running
#
# See: docs/claudecodebrowser-security-assessment.md

function ccb --description "Manage ClaudeCodeBrowser Firefox automation"
    set -l ccb_dir "$HOME/.claudecodebrowser"
    set -l pid_file "$ccb_dir/logs/server.pid"
    set -l log_dir "$ccb_dir/logs"

    if not test -d "$ccb_dir"
        echo "ClaudeCodeBrowser not installed. Run scripts/setup.sh or:"
        echo "  git clone https://github.com/nanogenomic/ClaudeCodeBrowser.git $ccb_dir"

1: # Granted Configuration Guide
2: 
3: This directory contains configuration for [Granted](https://granted.dev) - an AWS credential management tool with autocomplete, browser integration, and profile colors/icons.
4: 
5: ## What You Should See (UI Changes)
6: 
7: ### 1. Terminal Autocomplete ✅
8: When you type `assume` followed by space and Tab:
9: ```bash
10: assume <TAB>
11: ```
12: **Expected:** Shows your AWS profile names from `~/.aws/config`
13: **Not:** Granted command options or file names
14: 
15: ### 2. Firefox Browser Integration 🔥
16: When you run `assume production` and open AWS console:
17: - **Firefox opens** (not Chrome/Safari)
18: - **Colored container tabs** with icons (requires Firefox addon):
19:   - `production` → **Red briefcase** 🔴💼
20:   - `development` → **Green tree** 🟢🌳  
21:   - `staging` → **Yellow cart** 🟡🛒
22:   - `security` → **Purple fingerprint** 🟣👆
23: 
24: ### 3. Profile Ordering
25: Profiles appear in "frequently-used" order (most recent first)
26: 
27: ## Setup Instructions
28: 
29: ### Automatic Setup (Recommended)
30: The dotfiles setup script handles everything:
31: ```bash
32: ./scripts/setup.sh
33: ```
34: 
35: ### Manual Setup
36: 1. **Install Granted:**
37:    ```bash
38:    brew install granted
39:    ```
40: 
41: 2. **Create symlinks using stow:**
42:    ```bash
43:    cd ~/dotfiles
44:    stow . --adopt
45:    ```
46: 
47: 3. **Install Firefox addon:**
48:    - Visit: https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/granted/
49:    - Click "Add to Firefox"
50:    - Enable the addon
51: 
52: 4. **Reload Fish completions:**
53:    ```bash
54:    fish -c "source ~/.config/fish/completions/granted.fish"
55:    ```
56: 
57: ## Configuration Files
58: 
59: ### `config`
60: Main Granted configuration with:
61: - **Firefox** as default browser
62: - **Frequently-used** profile ordering  
63: - **Caching enabled** for performance
64: - **Telemetry disabled** for privacy
65: 
66: ### `firefox-profiles`
67: Maps AWS profiles to Firefox colors and icons:
68: ```
69: production=red:briefcase
70: development=green:tree
71: staging=yellow:cart
72: security=purple:fingerprint
73: ```
74: 
75: ## Available Colors & Icons
76: 
77: **Colors:** blue, turquoise, green, yellow, orange, red, pink, purple
78: **Icons:** fingerprint, briefcase, dollar, cart, circle, gift, vacation, food, fruit, pet, tree, chill
79: 
80: ## Usage Examples
81: 
82: ```bash
83: # Basic profile switching
84: assume production
85: 
86: # Open AWS console in Firefox with red briefcase container
87: assume production --console
88: 
89: # List available profiles
90: granted profiles list
91: 
92: # Configure profile colors
93: ./scripts/granted-setup.sh set-profile-color myprofile blue tree
94: 
95: # Test configuration
96: ./scripts/granted-setup.sh test-config
97: ```
98: 
99: ## Troubleshooting
100: 
101: ### Autocomplete Shows Wrong Items
102: **Problem:** `assume <TAB>` shows files or Granted commands instead of AWS profiles
103: **Solution:**
104: ```bash
105: # Reload Fish completions
106: fish -c "source ~/.config/fish/completions/granted.fish"
107: ```
108: 
109: ### No Colors/Icons in Firefox
110: **Problem:** Firefox tabs are plain, no colors or icons
111: **Solution:**
112: 1. Install the Granted Firefox addon: https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/granted/
113: 2. Ensure Firefox is set as default browser: `granted browser set firefox`
114: 3. Check profile mappings in `~/.granted/firefox-profiles`
115: 
116: ### Wrong Browser Opens
117: **Problem:** Chrome or Safari opens instead of Firefox
118: **Solution:**
119: ```bash
120: granted browser set firefox
121: ```
122: 
123: ### Profile Not Found
124: **Problem:** "Profile not found" errors
125: **Solution:**
126: 1. Check AWS configuration: `aws configure list-profiles`
127: 2. Verify AWS config file exists: `ls ~/.aws/config`
128: 3. Ensure profile names match exactly
129: 
130: ## Helper Scripts
131: 
132: Use `./scripts/granted-setup.sh` for management:
133: - `setup` - Initial configuration
134: - `set-profile-color PROFILE COLOR ICON` - Configure colors/icons
135: - `list-profiles` - Show configured profiles
136: - `test-config` - Validate setup
137: - `list-colors` - Show available options
138: 
139: ## Links
140: 
141: - **Documentation:** https://docs.commonfate.io/granted/
142: - **Firefox Addon:** https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/granted/
143: - **GitHub:** https://github.com/common-fate/granted

(End of file - total 143 lines)

phase_8_dotfiles() {
    [[ "$SKIP_DOTFILES" == "true" ]] && return 0
    [[ $(is_step_complete "dotfiles") == "true" ]] && return 0

    print_header "Phase 8: Dotfiles"

    # Clean corrupted git reflog entries before any git-dependent operations
    # Concurrent hooks (entire, beads) + fsmonitor can corrupt reflogs over time

1: #!/usr/bin/env bash

3: # dotfiles-manager.sh - Stow-based dotfile management

13: ensure_stow() {
14: 	if command_exists stow; then
15: 		return 0
16: 	fi
17: 
18: 	print_step "Installing stow..."
19: 
20: 	if pm_install stow; then
21: 		print_success "Stow installed"
22: 		return 0
23: 	else
24: 		print_warning "Failed to install stow, will use manual symlinking"
25: 		return 1
26: 	fi
27: }

29: # ============================================================================
30: # Stow-based Symlinking
31: # ============================================================================
32: 
33: detect_stow_conflicts() {
34: 	cd "$DOTFILES_ROOT" || return 1
35: 
36: 	# Run stow in simulation mode to detect conflicts
37: 	local conflicts
38: 	conflicts=$(stow . --no --verbose 2>&1 | grep -E "existing target|cannot stow" | head -10)
39: 
40: 	if [[ -n "$conflicts" ]]; then
41: 		return 0 # Conflicts found
42: 	fi
43: 	return 1 # No conflicts
44: }
45: 
46: backup_conflicting_files() {
47: 	print_step "Backing up conflicting files..."
48: 
49: 	local backup_dir
50: 	backup_dir="$HOME/.dotfiles-backup/$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)"
51: 	mkdir -p "$backup_dir"
52: 
53: 	cd "$DOTFILES_ROOT" || return 1
54: 
55: 	# Get list of conflicting files
56: 	local conflicts
57: 	conflicts=$(stow . --no --verbose 2>&1 | grep "existing target" | sed -E 's/.*existing target (.*) since.*/\1/' | sed 's/^[ \t]*//')
58: 
59: 	if [[ -z "$conflicts" ]]; then
60: 		log_verbose "No files to backup"
61: 		return 0
62: 	fi
63: 
64: 	# Backup each conflicting file
65: 	while IFS= read -r file; do
66: 		if [[ -n "$file" && -e "$HOME/$file" && ! -L "$HOME/$file" ]]; then
67: 			local backup_path="$backup_dir/$file"
68: 			mkdir -p "$(dirname "$backup_path")"
69: 			cp -a "$HOME/$file" "$backup_path"
70: 			log_verbose "Backed up: $file"
71: 		fi
72: 	done <<<"$conflicts"
73: 
74: 	print_success "Backed up conflicting files to: $backup_dir"
75: 	return 0
76: }
77: 
78: stow_dotfiles() {
79: 	print_header "Symlinking Dotfiles"
80: 
81: 	cd "$DOTFILES_ROOT" || return 1
82: 
83: 	if ! ensure_stow; then
84: 		manual_symlink_dotfiles

86: 	fi
87: 
88: 	print_step "Running stow..."
89: 
90: 	if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
91: 		stow . --no --verbose
92: 		return 0
93: 	fi
94:

104: 	fi

108:

130: 		mkdir -p "$HOME/.ssh"
131: 		chmod 700 "$HOME/.ssh"
132: 
133: 		if [[ -f "$HOME/.ssh/config" ]] && [[ ! -L "$HOME/.ssh/config" ]]; then
134: 			# Backup existing config

140: 		chmod 600 "$HOME/.ssh/config"
141: 		print_success "SSH config linked from dotfiles"
142: 	fi
143: }

145: # ============================================================================

152: 	local linked=0

154:

158: 		[[ "$file" =~ ^\.(git|stow-local-ignore)$ ]] && continue
159:

161: 		((linked++)) || true

163:

167: 		for dir in .config/*; do
168: 			[[ ! -d "$dir" ]] && continue
169: 			safe_symlink "$(pwd)/$dir" "$HOME/$dir"
170: 			((linked++)) || true
171: 		done
172: 	fi

175: }

content.summary:
User asked: “Is our Firefox config defined in our dotfiles? If so is it running as part of our setup.sh?” Investigation was read-only.

Findings:
- There is no full Firefox browser profile/config managed in dotfiles: no tracked/stowed Firefox profile tree was found, and glob searches found no `**/Firefox/**`, `**/firefox/**`, `**/.mozilla/**`, `**/user.js`, or `**/policies.json` under `/Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles`.
- `homebrew/Brewfile` has no `firefox` cask. It does include `brew "granted"` at line 92, and GUI casks near lines 295-298 only include AeroSpace, WezTerm, and session-manager-plugin.
- Firefox-related dotfiles do exist under `~dotfiles/.granted/` as Granted/AWS browser integration config, not as native Firefox preferences:
  - `/Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.granted/config` lines 1-3: `DefaultBrowser = "FIREFOX"`, `CustomBrowserPath = "/Applications/Firefox.app/Contents/MacOS/firefox"`, `CustomSSOBrowserPath = ""`; keyring backend is `keychain`.
  - `/Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.granted/firefox-profiles` maps AWS profile names to Firefox container tab colors/icons for the Granted Firefox addon. Examples: `production=red:briefcase`, `prod=red:briefcase`, `development=green:tree`, `dev=green:tree`, `security=purple:fingerprint`, `petlab=pink:pet`, `management=blue:briefcase`, `AWSAdministratorAccess-154805902702=red:fingerprint`, `default=blue:circle`.
  - `/Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.granted/README.md` documents the Granted Firefox integration and says `./scripts/setup.sh` handles automatic setup, but the actual `setup.sh` check showed no explicit Granted setup invocation.
- `/Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/scripts/setup/granted-setup.sh` is a helper script for Granted config. It sets `GRANTED_CONFIG_DIR="$HOME/.granted"`, `FIREFOX_PROFILES_FILE="$GRANTED_CONFIG_DIR/firefox-profiles"`, and `DOTFILES_CONFIG_DIR="$HOME/dotfiles/.granted"`. Its `setup_granted()` function checks `granted` exists, creates `~/.granted`, copies `$DOTFILES_CONFIG_DIR/config` to `~/.granted/config`, copies `$DOTFILES_CONFIG_DIR/firefox-profiles` to `~/.granted/firefox-profiles`, and generates Fish completions with `granted completion --shell fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/granted.fish`. However, searching `/Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/scripts/setup.sh` for `.granted`, `granted-setup`, `setup_granted`, `Granted`, and `firefox-profiles` found no matches; so this helper is not explicitly run by setup.sh.
- `/Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/scripts/setup.sh` does run `phase_8_dotfiles()` which calls `stow_dotfiles` at line 1666 unless `--skip-dotfiles` is used or the `dotfiles` step is already marked complete. `stow_dotfiles()` is defined in `/Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/scripts/lib/dotfiles-manager.sh` and executes `stow . --adopt --verbose` from `$DOTFILES_ROOT`. In dry-run it runs `stow . --no --verbose`.
- `.stow-local-ignore` was read: it does not ignore `.granted`, Firefox, Granted, or `firefox-profiles`; it ignores docs, scripts, homebrew, `.config/home-manager`, `.config/nvim`, `.ssh`, `.claude/settings.json`, generated artifacts, etc. Therefore `.granted/config` and `.granted/firefox-profiles` should be managed by the generic stow phase of setup.sh, but only through dotfile symlinking, not by executing `scripts/setup/granted-setup.sh`.
- Other Firefox references found:
  - `/Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.config/fish/functions/assume.fish` uses Granted Firefox containers when invoked as `assume -c <profile>`: it calls `granted console --firefox` with specific colors/icons/container names for `labs`, `logging`, `security`, `management`, `petlab`, `prod`, else `--container-name $profile`. This consumes Granted/Firefox integration but does not configure Firefox itself.
  - `/Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.config/fish/functions/ccb.fish` is a wrapper for ClaudeCodeBrowser Firefox automation, using runtime dir `$HOME/.claudecodebrowser`; it references the Firefox extension URL and setup.sh/manual clone instructions. It is not native Firefox preferences.
  - `/Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-url-handler.sh` opens URLs via `open -a Firefox`; not browser config.
- A noisy repo-wide grep initially found many Firefox hits in `.entire/opencode/sse/events.jsonl` and `.granted/log`; these were runtime/session logs and not source config. Narrowed searches to `scripts`, `.config`, `homebrew`, and direct file reads were used for conclusions.

Answer to give: “Partially. We have Granted/AWS Firefox integration config in `.granted/config` and `.granted/firefox-profiles`, and Fish helpers that use `granted console --firefox`. We do not have native Firefox profile preferences such as `user.js`, `profiles.ini`, `policies.json`, or a Firefox profile directory in dotfiles. `setup.sh` does not explicitly run `scripts/setup/granted-setup.sh`; it only includes this config indirectly via Phase 8 `stow_dotfiles`, because `.granted` is not ignored by `.stow-local-ignore`. Also Firefox itself is not installed by the Brewfile.”

metadata.preview:
phase_4_cloud_tools() {
    [[ "$SKIP_PACKAGES" == "true" ]] && return 0
    [[ $(is_step_complete "cloud_tools") == "true" ]] && return 0

    print_header "Phase 4: Cloud & Infrastructure Tools"

    install_packages_from_profile "$PROFILE" "cloud"
    install_packages_from_profile "$PROFILE" "kubernetes"
    install_packages_from_profile "$PROFILE" "containers"
    install_packages_from_profile "$PROFILE" "security"
    install_packages_from_profile "$PROFILE" "monitoring"
    install_packages_from_profile "$PROFILE" "network"
    install_packages_from_profile "$PROFILE" "performance"
    install_packages_from_profile "$PROFILE" "data"

    if [[ "$DRY_RUN" == "true" ]]; then
        print_warning "DRY RUN: Skipping cloud tool post-install steps and MCP configuration"
        mark_step_complete "cloud_tools"
        return 0
    fi

phase_8_dotfiles() {
    [[ "$SKIP_DOTFILES" == "true" ]] && return 0
    [[ $(is_step_complete "dotfiles") == "true" ]] && return 0

    print_header "Phase 8: Dotfiles"

    # Clean corrupted git reflog entries before any git-dependent operations
    # Concurrent hooks (entire, beads) + fsmonitor can corrupt reflogs over time
    if command_exists git && [[ -d "$DOTFILES_ROOT/.git" ]]; then
        print_step "Cleaning git reflog..."
        git -C "$DOTFILES_ROOT" reflog expire --expire=now --all 2>/dev/null &&
            log_verbose "Git reflog cleaned" ||
            log_verbose "Git reflog cleanup skipped"
    fi

<content>
1: #!/bin/bash
2: # Granted Setup and Management Helper Script
3: # Provides utilities for managing Granted AWS profile colors, icons, and configurations
4: 
5: set -e
6: 
7: # Color codes for output
8: RED='\033[0;31m'
9: GREEN='\033[0;32m'
10: YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
11: BLUE='\033[0;34m'
12: PURPLE='\033[0;35m'
13: CYAN='\033[0;36m'
14: NC='\033[0m' # No Color
15: 
16: # Configuration paths
17: GRANTED_CONFIG_DIR="$HOME/.granted"
18: FIREFOX_PROFILES_FILE="$GRANTED_CONFIG_DIR/firefox-profiles"
19: DOTFILES_CONFIG_DIR="$HOME/dotfiles/.granted"
20: 
21: # Available colors and icons
22: COLORS=(blue turquoise green yellow orange red pink purple)
23: ICONS=(fingerprint briefcase dollar cart circle gift vacation food fruit pet tree chill)
24: 
25: print_usage() {
26:     cat << EOF
27: Granted Setup and Management Helper
28: 
29: Usage: $0 [command] [options]
30: 
31: Commands:
32:     setup                       - Initial setup and configuration
33:     set-profile-color PROFILE COLOR ICON - Set Firefox color/icon for profile
34:     list-profiles              - List configured Firefox profiles
35:     list-colors                - Show available colors and icons
36:     test-config               - Test Granted configuration
37:     backup-config             - Backup current configuration
38:     restore-config            - Restore configuration from dotfiles
39:     help                      - Show this help message
40: 
41: Examples:
42:     $0 setup
43:     $0 set-profile-color production red briefcase
44:     $0 set-profile-color development green tree
45:     $0 list-profiles
46:     $0 test-config
47: 
48: Available Colors: $(printf '%s ' "${COLORS[@]}")
49: Available Icons:  $(printf '%s ' "${ICONS[@]}")
50: EOF
51: }
52: 
53: log_info() {
54:     echo -e "${BLUE}[INFO]${NC} $1"
55: }
56: 
57: log_success() {
58:     echo -e "${GREEN}[SUCCESS]${NC} $1"
59: }
60: 
61: log_warning() {
62:     echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARNING]${NC} $1"
63: }
64: 
65: log_error() {
66:     echo -e "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} $1"
67: }
68: 
69: validate_color() {
70:     local color="$1"
71:     for valid_color in "${COLORS[@]}"; do
72:         if [[ "$color" == "$valid_color" ]]; then
73:             return 0
74:         fi
75:     done
76:     return 1
77: }
78: 
79: validate_icon() {
80:     local icon="$1"
81:     for valid_icon in "${ICONS[@]}"; do
82:         if [[ "$icon" == "$valid_icon" ]]; then
83:             return 0
84:         fi
85:     done
86:     return 1
87: }
88: 
89: setup_granted() {
90:     log_info "Setting up Granted configuration..."
91: 
92:     # Check if Granted is installed
93:     if ! command -v granted &> /dev/null; then
94:         log_error "Granted is not installed. Please install it first with: brew install granted"
95:         exit 1
96:     fi
97: 
98:     # Create config directory
99:     mkdir -p "$GRANTED_CONFIG_DIR"
100: 
101:     # Copy configuration files from dotfiles
102:     if [[ -f "$DOTFILES_CONFIG_DIR/config" ]]; then
103:         log_info "Installing Granted configuration..."
104:         cp "$DOTFILES_CONFIG_DIR/config" "$GRANTED_CONFIG_DIR/config"
105:         log_success "Granted configuration installed"
106:     else
107:         log_warning "Granted config file not found in dotfiles"
108:     fi
109: 
110:     if [[ -f "$DOTFILES_CONFIG_DIR/firefox-profiles" ]]; then
111:         log_info "Installing Firefox profiles configuration..."
112:         cp "$DOTFILES_CONFIG_DIR/firefox-profiles" "$FIREFOX_PROFILES_FILE"
113:         log_success "Firefox profiles configuration installed"
114:     else
115:         log_warning "Firefox profiles config not found in dotfiles"
116:     fi
117: 
118:     # Generate shell completions
119:     if command -v fish &> /dev/null; then
120:         log_info "Generating Fish shell completions..."
121:         mkdir -p "$HOME/.config/fish/completions"
122:         granted completion --shell fish > "$HOME/.config/fish/completions/granted.fish" 2>/dev/null || true
123:         log_success "Fish completions generated"
124:     fi
125: 
126:     if command -v zsh &> /dev/null; then
127:         log_info "Zsh completions will be loaded automatically"
128:     fi
129: 
130:     log_success "Granted setup complete!"
131:     log_info "You can now use 'assume <profile>' to switch AWS profiles"
132:     log_info "Firefox profiles will use colors and icons as configured"
133: }
134: 
135: set_profile_color() {
136:     local profile="$1"
137:     local color="$2"
138:     local icon="$3"
139: 
140:     if [[ -z "$profile" || -z "$color" || -z "$icon" ]]; then
141:         log_error "Usage: set-profile-color PROFILE COLOR ICON"
142:         return 1
143:     fi
144: 
145:     if ! validate_color "$color"; then
146:         log_error "Invalid color: $color"
147:         log_info "Available colors: $(printf '%s ' "${COLORS[@]}")"
148:         return 1
149:     fi
150: 
151:     if ! validate_icon "$icon"; then
152:         log_error "Invalid icon: $icon"
153:         log_info "Available icons: $(printf '%s ' "${ICONS[@]}")"
154:         return 1
155:     fi
156: 
157:     # Ensure Firefox profiles file exists
158:     if [[ ! -f "$FIREFOX_PROFILES_FILE" ]]; then
159:         log_warning "Firefox profiles file not found, creating..."
160:         mkdir -p "$GRANTED_CONFIG_DIR"
161:         touch "$FIREFOX_PROFILES_FILE"
162:     fi
163: 
164:     # Remove existing entry for this profile
165:     grep -v "^$profile=" "$FIREFOX_PROFILES_FILE" > "$FIREFOX_PROFILES_FILE.tmp" 2>/dev/null || true
166: 
167:     # Add new entry
168:     echo "$profile=$color:$icon" >> "$FIREFOX_PROFILES_FILE.tmp"
169: 
170:     # Sort and replace
171:     sort "$FIREFOX_PROFILES_FILE.tmp" > "$FIREFOX_PROFILES_FILE"
172:     rm "$FIREFOX_PROFILES_FILE.tmp"
173: 
174:     log_success "Set $profile to use $color color with $icon icon"
175: }
176: 
177: list_profiles() {
178:     log_info "Configured Firefox profiles:"
179: 
180:     if [[ ! -f "$FIREFOX_PROFILES_FILE" ]]; then
181:         log_warning "No Firefox profiles configuration found"
182:         return 1
183:     fi
184: 
185:     while IFS='=' read -r profile config; do
186:         if [[ -n "$profile" && ! "$profile" =~ ^# ]]; then
187:             IFS=':' read -r color icon <<< "$config"
188:             printf "  %-20s %s%s%s (icon: %s)\n" "$profile" "$color" "$color" "${NC}" "$icon"
189:         fi
190:     done < "$FIREFOX_PROFILES_FILE"
191: }
192: 
193: list_colors() {
194:     log_info "Available Firefox colors and icons:"
195:     echo
196:     echo "Colors:"
197:     for color in "${COLORS[@]}"; do
198:         printf "  %s\n" "$color"
199:     done
200:     echo
201:     echo "Icons:"
202:     for icon in "${ICONS[@]}"; do
203:         printf "  %s\n" "$icon"
204:     done
205: }
206: 
207: test_config() {
208:     log_info "Testing Granted configuration..."
209: 
210:     # Check if Granted is installed
211:     if ! command -v granted &> /dev/null; then
212:         log_error "Granted is not installed"
213:         return 1
214:     fi
215:

<content>
1: # Granted Configuration Guide
2: 
3: This directory contains configuration for [Granted](https://granted.dev) - an AWS credential management tool with autocomplete, browser integration, and profile colors/icons.
4: 
5: ## What You Should See (UI Changes)
6: 
7: ### 1. Terminal Autocomplete ✅
8: When you type `assume` followed by space and Tab:
9: ```bash
10: assume <TAB>
11: ```
12: **Expected:** Shows your AWS profile names from `~/.aws/config`
13: **Not:** Granted command options or file names
14: 
15: ### 2. Firefox Browser Integration 🔥
16: When you run `assume production` and open AWS console:
17: - **Firefox opens** (not Chrome/Safari)
18: - **Colored container tabs** with icons (requires Firefox addon):
19:   - `production` → **Red briefcase** 🔴💼
20:   - `development` → **Green tree** 🟢🌳  
21:   - `staging` → **Yellow cart** 🟡🛒
22:   - `security` → **Purple fingerprint** 🟣👆
23: 
24: ### 3. Profile Ordering
25: Profiles appear in "frequently-used" order (most recent first)
26: 
27: ## Setup Instructions
28: 
29: ### Automatic Setup (Recommended)
30: The dotfiles setup script handles everything:
31: ```bash
32: ./scripts/setup.sh
33: ```
34: 
35: ### Manual Setup
36: 1. **Install Granted:**
37:    ```bash
38:    brew install granted
39:    ```
40: 
41: 2. **Create symlinks using stow:**
42:    ```bash
43:    cd ~/dotfiles
44:    stow . --adopt
45:    ```
46: 
47: 3. **Install Firefox addon:**
48:    - Visit: https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/granted/
49:    - Click "Add to Firefox"
50:    - Enable the addon
51: 
52: 4. **Reload Fish completions:**
53:    ```bash
54:    fish -c "source ~/.config/fish/completions/granted.fish"
55:    ```
56: 
57: ## Configuration Files
58: 
59: ### `config`
60: Main Granted configuration with:
61: - **Firefox** as default browser
62: - **Frequently-used** profile ordering  
63: - **Caching enabled** for performance
64: - **Telemetry disabled** for privacy
65: 
66: ### `firefox-profiles`
67: Maps AWS profiles to Firefox colors and icons:
68: ```
69: production=red:briefcase
70: development=green:tree
71: staging=yellow:cart
72: security=purple:fingerprint
73: ```
74: 
75: ## Available Colors & Icons
76: 
77: **Colors:** blue, turquoise, green, yellow, orange, red, pink, purple
78: **Icons:** fingerprint, briefcase, dollar, cart, circle, gift, vacation, food, fruit, pet, tree, chill
79: 
80: ## Usage Examples
81: 
82: ```bash
83: # Basic profile switching
84: assume production
85: 
86: # Open AWS console in Firefox with red briefcase container
87: assume production --console
88: 
89: # List available profiles
90: granted profiles list
91: 
92: # Configure profile colors
93: ./scripts/granted-setup.sh set-profile-color myprofile blue tree
94: 
95: # Test configuration
96: ./scripts/granted-setup.sh test-config
97: ```
98: 
99: ## Troubleshooting
100: 
101: ### Autocomplete Shows Wrong Items
102: **Problem:** `assume <TAB>` shows files or Granted commands instead of AWS profiles
103: **Solution:**
104: ```bash
105: # Reload Fish completions
106: fish -c "source ~/.config/fish/completions/granted.fish"
107: ```
108: 
109: ### No Colors/Icons in Firefox
110: **Problem:** Firefox tabs are plain, no colors or icons
111: **Solution:**
112: 1. Install the Granted Firefox addon: https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/granted/
113: 2. Ensure Firefox is set as default browser: `granted browser set firefox`
114: 3. Check profile mappings in `~/.granted/firefox-profiles`
115: 
116: ### Wrong Browser Opens
117: **Problem:** Chrome or Safari opens instead of Firefox
118: **Solution:**
119: ```bash
120: granted browser set firefox
121: ```
122: 
123: ### Profile Not Found
124: **Problem:** "Profile not found" errors
125: **Solution:**
126: 1. Check AWS configuration: `aws configure list-profiles`
127: 2. Verify AWS config file exists: `ls ~/.aws/config`
128: 3. Ensure profile names match exactly
129: 
130: ## Helper Scripts
131: 
132: Use `./scripts/granted-setup.sh` for management:
133: - `setup` - Initial configuration
134: - `set-profile-color PROFILE COLOR ICON` - Configure colors/icons
135: - `list-profiles` - Show configured profiles
136: - `test-config` - Validate setup
137: - `list-colors` - Show available options
138: 
139: ## Links
140: 
141: - **Documentation:** https://docs.commonfate.io/granted/
142: - **Firefox Addon:** https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/granted/
143: - **GitHub:** https://github.com/common-fate/granted

(End of file - total 143 lines)
</content>

function assume --description "Assume AWS role using Granted"
    # Handle console flag for Firefox containers
    if test "$argv[1]" = -c
        set -l profile $argv[2]
        if test -n "$profile"
            # First assume the profile using bash with suppressed interactive prompts
            set -l env_vars (printf 'n\n' | bash -c "source /opt/homebrew/bin/assume $profile >/dev/null 2>&1 && env | grep -E '^(AWS_|GRANTED_)'")
            _parse_granted_env $env_vars

            # Then open console with profile-specific container names and colors
            switch $profile
                case labs
                    granted console --firefox --color green --icon tree --container-name labs
                case logging
                    granted console --firefox --color purple --icon circle --container-name logging
                case security
                    granted console --firefox --color purple --icon fingerprint --container-name security
                case management

1: #!/usr/bin/env bash
2: 
3: # package-manager.sh - Abstract package manager interface
4: # Defines the common interface that OS-specific implementations must provide
5: 
6: # Source common utilities
7: # shellcheck source=./common.sh
8: source "$SCRIPT_DIR/lib/common.sh"
9: 
10: # ============================================================================
11: # Abstract Interface - Must be implemented by OS-specific modules
12: # ============================================================================
13: 
14: # pm_init() - Initialize package manager (detect distro, check permissions)
15: # pm_update() - Update package cache/repositories
16: # pm_install() - Install a single package
17: # pm_install_batch() - Install multiple packages
18: # pm_is_installed() - Check if package is installed
19: # pm_search() - Search for package
20: # pm_remove() - Remove package
21: # pm_cleanup() - Clean package manager cache
22: # pm_map_package_name() - Map generic name to OS-specific name
23: 
24: # ============================================================================
25: # OS-Specific Module Loading
26: # ============================================================================
27: 
28: load_package_manager() {
29:     local os=${DETECTED_OS:-$(detect_os)}
30:     local pm_module="$SCRIPT_DIR/os/$os/package-manager.sh"
31: 
32:     if [[ ! -f "$pm_module" ]]; then
33:         print_error "Package manager module not found for OS: $os"
34:         print_error "Expected: $pm_module"
35:         return 1
36:     fi
37: 
38:     # shellcheck source=/dev/null
39:     source "$pm_module"
40: 
41:     # Initialize the package manager
42:     if ! pm_init; then
43:         print_error "Failed to initialize package manager"
44:         return 1
45:     fi
46: 
47:     print_success "Package manager initialized: $os"
48:     log "Package manager module loaded: $pm_module"
49: }
50: 
51: # ============================================================================
52: # High-Level Package Operations
53: # ============================================================================
54: 
55: install_package_group() {
56:     local group_name=$1
57:     shift
58:     local packages=("$@")
59: 
60:     print_step "Installing $group_name packages..."
61:     echo ""
62: 
63:     local to_install=()
64:     local skipped=()
65: 
66:     # First pass: check what's already installed
67:     for package in "${packages[@]}"; do
68:         if pm_is_installed "$package"; then
69:             skipped+=("$package")
70:             print_success "Already installed: $package"
71:         else
72:             to_install+=("$package")
73:         fi
74:     done
75: 
76:     local installed=()
77:     local failed=()
78: 
79:     # Batch install missing packages (single brew invocation resolves deps once)
80:     if [[ ${#to_install[@]} -gt 0 ]] && declare -f pm_install_batch >/dev/null 2>&1; then
81:         if pm_install_batch "${to_install[@]}"; then
82:             installed=("${to_install[@]}")
83:             for pkg in "${to_install[@]}"; do
84:                 print_success "Installed: $pkg"
85:             done
86:         else
87:             # Batch failed — fall back to individual installs for granular error reporting
88:             for package in "${to_install[@]}"; do
89:                 if pm_install "$package"; then
90:                     installed+=("$package")
91:                     print_success "Installed: $package"
92:                 else
93:                     failed+=("$package")
94:                     print_warning "Failed to install: $package"
95:                 fi
96:             done
97:         fi
98:     else
99:         # No batch function available — install individually
100:         for package in "${to_install[@]}"; do
101:             if pm_install "$package"; then
102:                 installed+=("$package")
103:                 print_success "Installed: $package"
104:             else
105:                 failed+=("$package")
106:                 print_warning "Failed to install: $package"
107:             fi
108:         done
109:     fi
110: 
111:     # Phase summary
112:     echo ""
113:     print_step "[$group_name] Summary:"
114:     echo "  • Newly installed: ${#installed[@]}"
115:     echo "  • Already installed: ${#skipped[@]}"
116:     if [[ ${#failed[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
117:         echo "  • Failed: ${#failed[@]}"
118:     fi
119:     echo ""
120: 
121:     if [[ ${#installed[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
122:         log "Installed ${#installed[@]} packages: ${installed[*]}"
123:     fi
124: 
125:     if [[ ${#failed[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
126:         log "Failed to install ${#failed[@]} packages: ${failed[*]}"
127:         # In Docker/CI environments (FORCE_ONLINE=true), don't fail on package errors
128:         # This allows setup to continue even if OS-specific packages (like mas) fail
129:         if [[ "${FORCE_ONLINE:-false}" != "true" ]]; then
130:             return 1
131:         fi
132:     fi
133: 
134:     return 0
135: }
136: 
137: ensure_package_installed() {
138:     local package=$1
139:     local description=${2:-$package}
140: 
141:     if pm_is_installed "$package"; then
142:         log_verbose "$description is already installed"
143:         return 0
144:     fi
145: 
146:     print_step "Installing $description..."
147: 
148:     if pm_install "$package"; then
149:         print_success "Installed: $description"
150:         return 0
151:     else
152:         print_error "Failed to install: $description"
153:         return 1
154:     fi
155: }
156: 
157: # ============================================================================
158: # Package Lists from Profiles
159: # ============================================================================
160: 
161: get_package_list_from_profile() {
162:     local profile=$1
163:     local section=$2
164: 
165:     local profile_file="$SCRIPT_DIR/profiles/${profile}.conf"
166: 
167:     if [[ ! -f "$profile_file" ]]; then
168:         print_error "Profile not found: $profile"
169:         return 1
170:     fi
171: 
172:     # Parse INI-style config for enabled packages in section
173:     local in_section=false
174:     local packages=()
175: 
176:     while IFS= read -r line; do
177:         # Skip comments and empty lines
178:         [[ "$line" =~ ^#.*$ || -z "$line" ]] && continue
179: 
180:         # Check for section header
181:         if [[ "$line" =~ ^\[(.+)\]$ ]]; then
182:             if [[ "${BASH_REMATCH[1]}" == "$section" ]]; then
183:                 in_section=true
184:             else
185:                 in_section=false
186:             fi
187:             continue
188:         fi
189: 
190:         # If in target section, parse key=value
191:         if [[ "$in_section" == "true" && "$line" =~ ^([^=]+)=(.+)$ ]]; then
192:             local key="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
193:             local value="${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"
194: 
195:             # If value is "true", add package to list
196:             if [[ "$value" == "true" ]]; then
197:                 packages+=("$key")
198:             fi
199:         fi
200:     done <"$profile_file"
201: 
202:     # Return packages as space-separated string
203:     echo "${packages[*]}"
204: }
205: 
206: install_packages_from_profile() {
207:     local profile=$1
208:     local section=$2
209: 
210:     local packages=$(get_package_list_from_profile "$profile" "$section")
211: 
212:     if [[ -z "$packages" ]]; then
213:         print_step "No packages configured for [$section]"
214:         log_verbose "Skipping empty section: $section"
215:         return 0
216:     fi
217: 
218:     # Show package count
219:     local package_array=($packages)
220:     print_step "Found ${#package_array[@]} package(s) in [$section] to process"
221:     log_verbose "Package list: ${packages}"
222:     echo ""
223: 
224:     install_package_group "$section" $packages
225: }
226: 
227: # ============================================================================
228: # Validation
229: # ============================================================================
230: 
231: validate_package_manager() {
232:     local required_functions=(
233:         "pm_init"
234:         "pm_update"
235:         "pm_install"
236:         "pm_is_installed"
237:         "pm_map_package_name"
238:     )
239: 
240:     for func in "${required_functions[@]}"; do
241:         if ! declare -f "$func" >/dev/null; then
242:             print_error "Required function not implemented: $func"
243:             return 1
244:         fi
245:     done
246: 
247:     log_verbose "Package manager interface validation passed"
248:     return 0
249: }
250: 
251: # ============================================================================
252: # Initialize
253: # ============================================================================
254: 
255: init_package_manager() {
256:     log "Initializing package manager system..."
257: 
258:     # Load OS-specific package manager
259:     if ! load_package_manager; then

1: #!/usr/bin/env bash
2: 
3: # macOS Package Manager Implementation - Homebrew
4: # Implements the abstract package manager interface for macOS
5: 
6: # ============================================================================
7: # Homebrew Detection & Installation
8: # ============================================================================
9: 
10: ensure_homebrew() {
11:     if command_exists brew; then
12:         log_verbose "Homebrew already installed"
13:         return 0
14:     fi
15: 
16:     print_step "Installing Homebrew..."
17: 
18:     if /bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"; then
19:         # Add brew to PATH for Apple Silicon
20:         if [[ $(detect_arch) == "arm64" ]]; then
21:             eval "$(/opt/homebrew/bin/brew shellenv)"
22:         fi
23:         print_success "Homebrew installed"
24:         return 0
25:     else
26:         print_error "Failed to install Homebrew"
27:         return 1
28:     fi
29: }
30: 
31: # ============================================================================
32: # Package Manager Interface Implementation
33: # ============================================================================
34: 
35: pm_init() {
36:     ensure_homebrew || return 1
37: 
38:     export PACKAGE_MANAGER="homebrew"
39:     log "Package manager initialized: Homebrew"
40:     return 0
41: }
42: 
43: pm_update() {
44:     if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
45:         print_warning "DRY RUN: Would update Homebrew"
46:         return 0
47:     fi
48:     print_step "Updating Homebrew..."
49:     # Tolerate partial failures (e.g., network errors fetching metadata)
50:     brew update 2>&1 || log_verbose "Homebrew update completed with warnings"
51: }
52: 
53: pm_upgrade() {
54:     if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
55:         print_warning "DRY RUN: Would upgrade all Homebrew packages"
56:         return 0
57:     fi
58:     print_step "Upgrading Homebrew packages..."
59:     brew upgrade 2>&1 || log_verbose "Homebrew upgrade completed with warnings"
60: }
61: 
62: pm_install() {
63:     local package=$(pm_map_package_name "$1")
64: 
65:     if [[ -z "$package" ]]; then
66:         return 1
67:     fi
68: 
69:     if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
70:         print_warning "DRY RUN: Would install $package via brew"
71:         return 0
72:     fi
73:     brew install "$package" 2>&1 | grep -v "already installed" || return 0
74: }
75: 
76: pm_install_batch() {
77:     local packages=("$@")
78:     local mapped=()
79: 
80:     for pkg in "${packages[@]}"; do
81:         local name
82:         name=$(pm_map_package_name "$pkg")
83:         # Skip packages that map to empty (not available on this OS)
84:         [[ -n "$name" ]] && mapped+=("$name")
85:     done
86: 
87:     if [[ ${#mapped[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
88:         return 0
89:     fi
90: 
91:     if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
92:         print_warning "DRY RUN: Would install batch via brew: ${mapped[*]}"
93:         return 0
94:     fi
95:     brew install "${mapped[@]}" 2>&1 | grep -v "already installed" || return 0
96: }
97: 
98: pm_is_installed() {
99:     local package=$(pm_map_package_name "$1")
100:     brew list "$package" &>/dev/null
101: }
102: 
103: pm_search() {
104:     brew search "$1"
105: }
106: 
107: pm_remove() {
108:     local package=$(pm_map_package_name "$1")
109:     if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
110:         print_warning "DRY RUN: Would uninstall $package via brew"
111:         return 0
112:     fi
113:     brew uninstall "$package"
114: }
115: 
116: pm_cleanup() {
117:     if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-false}" == "true" ]]; then

state.title: scripts/os/macos/package-manager.sh

parts.text:
**Exploring Firefox config management**

I'm thinking about using GNU Stow for managing my home configurations, particularly related to Firefox. I wonder if I can integrate the Firefox profile using user.js in my home directory, even though the app bundle is outside of it. The profile paths seem complicated, but I might find a way to create a deterministic profile. I need to consider existing Firefox profiles and whether I should preserve them, especially if conflicts arise during adoption.

1: function claude-sub --description "Manage Claude subscription profiles"
2:     # Usage: claude-sub <command> [args...]
3:     #
4:     # Manages multiple Claude Max subscription profiles. Each profile gets its
5:     # own config directory (~/.claude-<name>) with shared config symlinked from
6:     # the primary ~/.claude/ and isolated credentials/runtime data.
7:     #
8:     # Commands:
9:     #   setup <name> [--org-uuid UUID]  Create profile with shared config
10:     #   list                            List profiles with org/plan info
11:     #   current                         Show which profile is active
12:     #   login <name>                    Re-authenticate a profile
13:     #   help                            Show help
14: 
15:     set -l cmd $argv[1]
16:     set -l rest
17:     if test (count $argv) -gt 1
18:         set rest $argv[2..]
19:     end
20: 
21:     switch "$cmd"
22:         case setup
23:             _claude_sub_setup $rest
24: 
25:         case list ls
26:             _claude_sub_list
27: 
28:         case current
29:             _claude_sub_current
30: 
31:         case login
32:             if test (count $rest) -eq 0
33:                 echo "Usage: claude-sub login <name>"
34:                 return 1
35:             end
36:             _claude_sub_login $rest[1]
37: 
38:         case help --help -h ''
39:             echo "claude-sub - Manage Claude su...

Does anything like this alraady exist online?" I investigated the repo and public tools, then answered that this is feasible and that the repo already contains most of the local primitives needed. Key repo findings: `claude-sub` manages multiple Claude profiles under `~/.claude-<name>/` in `.config/fish/functions/claude-sub.fish`; `scripts/ticket-queue/claude-usage.sh` can query Claude usage per profile via `--config-dir` and returns utilization/reset data for 5-hour, 7-day Sonnet, and 7-day Opus windows; `codex-accounts` manages multiple Codex/OpenAI accounts in `~/.codex/accounts/<name>/auth.json`; `scripts/opencode/usage-check.sh` probes OpenAI/Codex availability and returns availability / usage-limit / auth-expired style results but not detailed percentage/reset windows; docs in `docs/codex-account-rotation.md` describe the multi-account rotation model. Additional repo exploration found relevant tmux/dashboard surfaces: `.tmux.conf` has existing popup bindings (`S`, `T`) and launch bindings for Claude/OpenCode (`a`, `O`); `.config/fish/config.fish` already contains aliases for `claude-sub` and worktree commands; `gwt-dashboard.fish` is just a tiny wrapper to `scripts/agent-dashboard.sh`; `scripts/otel/grafana/dashboards/claude-code.json` exists but is about telemetry/cost/token metrics rather than subscription-account overview. ...

10: 
11: # Obsidian Session Synthesis
12: 
13: Per-session synthesis of Claude Code work into the Obsidian vault. Triggered at multiple lifecycle boundaries to capture context before it's lost (compaction, worktree cleanup, ticket completion, etc.).
14: 
15: ## Architecture
16: 
17: Single backing script (`scripts/obsidian/session-synthesize.sh`) with one Fish wrapper (`obsidian-session-sync` / `oss`). All callers — hooks, skills, scripts — funnel through these two surfaces.
18: 
19: ## Trigger Surface
20: 
21: | Trigger | Reason tag | Force? | Wired in |
22: |---------|------------|--------|----------|
23: | `Stop` hook (every Claude turn end) | `stop` | no | `.claude/settings.json` |
24: | `SessionEnd` hook | `session-end` | yes | `.claude/settings.json` |
25: | `PreCompact` hook | `pre-compact` | yes | `.claude/settings.json` |
26: | tmux `prefix+x` (kill pane) | `worktree-cleanup` | yes | `scripts/tmux/tmux-kill-pane-cleanup.sh` → `tmux-worktree-cleanup.sh` |
27: | tmux `prefix+X` (kill window) | `worktree-cleanup` | yes | `.tmux.conf:405` → `tmux-worktree-cleanup.sh` |
28: | `ticket-execute --complete` | `ticket-done` | yes | `scripts/...

130: 
131: ### Claude Code Settings & Security
132: 
133: **Settings Hierarchy** (higher overrides lower): Managed → Local (`.claude/settings.local.json`) → Project (`.claude/settings.json`) → User (`~/.claude/settings.json`)
134: 
135: **CLAUDE.md Hierarchy**: Claude Code walks UP from CWD, loading all `CLAUDE.md` / `.claude/CLAUDE.md` files. Does NOT require a git repo. A shared `~/work/CLAUDE.md` applies to all projects within `~/work/`; deeper files override shallower ones. `@import` supports up to 5 hops. Exclude with `claudeMdExcludes` in settings. Guide: `docs/claudemd-hierarchy.md`. Template: `templates/workspace-CLAUDE.md`.
136: 
137: **Key configs** (`~/.claude.json`):
138: - Sandbox: `autoAllowBashIfSandboxed: true`, `excludedCommands: ["docker", "colima"]`
139: - Attribution: `commit: ""`, `pr: ""` (suppress AI trailers)
140: - Permission rules: deny → ask → allow (first match wins)
141: 
142: **Model**: Opus 4.7 default via project settings (`model: claude-opus-4-7`), `CLAUDE_CODE_EFFORT_LEVEL=max`, `--effort max` CLI flag on launch commands, `/model opusplan` for plan→execute split.
143: **Fullscreen Stability**: Default `CLAUDE_CODE_NO_FLICKER` to `0` in host shells and devcontainers to avoid redraw issues from inherited environments. Enable fullscreen rendering per session with `/tui fullscreen`, or flip `CLAUDE_CODE_NO_FLICKER=1` only as a temporary troubleshooting override on older Claude Code versions.

1: -- Nix-aware LSP Configuration
2: -- Prioritizes Nix-provided LSPs (no Mason fallback)
3: -- This allows per-project LSP versioning via Nix flakes
4: 
5: -- Helper function to get the full path of a command
6: -- Uses vim.fn.exepath (built-in, no subprocess spawn)
7: local function get_command_path(cmd)
8:   local path = vim.fn.exepath(cmd)
9:   return path ~= "" and path or nil
10: end
11: 
12: -- Helper function to get command path for LSP
13: -- Returns { full_path, ...extra_args } or nil if binary not found
14: -- extra_args should match the upstream nvim-lspconfig cmd (e.g. "--stdio", "start", "serve")
15: local function get_lsp_cmd(nix_cmd, ...)
16:   local full_path = get_command_path(nix_cmd)
17:   if not full_path then
18:     return nil
19:   end
20:   local cmd = { full_path }
21:   for i = 1, select("#", ...) do
22:     cmd[#cmd + 1] = select(i, ...)
23:   end
24:   return cmd
25: end

+
+local function copilot_sign_in(bufnr, client)
+  client:request("signIn", vim.empty_dict(), function(err, result)
+    if err then
+      vim.notify(err.message, vim.log.levels.ERROR)
+      return
+    end
+
+    if result.command then
+      local code = result.userCode
+      vim.fn.setreg("+", code)
+      vim.fn.setreg("*", code)
+
+      local continue = vim.fn.confirm(
+        "Copied your one-time code to clipboard.\nOpen the browser to complete the sign-in process?",
+        "&Yes\n&No"
+      )
+
+      if continue == 1 then
+        client:exec_cmd(result.command, { bufnr = bufnr }, function(cmd_err, cmd_result)
+          if cmd_err then
+            vim.notify(cmd_err.message, vim.log.levels.ERROR)
+            return
+          end
+
+          if cmd_result.status == "OK" then
+            vim.notify("Signed in as " .. cmd_result.user .. ".")
+          end
+        end)
+      end
+    end
+
+    if result.status == "PromptUserDeviceFlow" then
+      vim.notify("Enter your one-time code " .. result.userCode .. " in " .. result.verificationUri)
+    elseif result.status == "AlreadySignedIn" then
+      vim.notify("Already signed in as " .. result.user .. ".")
+    end
+  end)
+end
+

+  client:request("signOut", vim.empty_dict(), function(err, result)
+    if err then

1: -- Nix-aware LSP Configuration
2: -- Prioritizes Nix-provided LSPs (no Mason fallback)
3: -- This allows per-project LSP versioning via Nix flakes
4: 
5: -- Helper function to get the full path of a command
6: -- Uses vim.fn.exepath (built-in, no subprocess spawn)
7: local function get_command_path(cmd)
8:   local path = vim.fn.exepath(cmd)
9:   return path ~= "" and path or nil
10: end
11: 
12: -- Helper function to get command path for LSP
13: -- Returns { full_path, ...extra_args } or nil if binary not found
14: -- extra_args should match the upstream nvim-lspconfig cmd (e.g. "--stdio", "start", "serve")
15: local function get_lsp_cmd(nix_cmd, ...)
16:   local full_path = get_command_path(nix_cmd)
17:   if not full_path then
18:     return nil
19:   end
20:   local cmd = { full_path }
21:   for i = 1, select("#", ...) do
22:     cmd[#cmd + 1] = select(i, ...)
23:   end
24:   return cmd
25: end
26: 
27: local function get_twig_language_server_extension_path(cmd_path)
28:   local install_root = vim.fs.dirname(vim.fs.dirname(cmd_path))
29:   local wasm_source = vim.fs.joinpath(
30:     install_root,
31:     "lib",
32:     "node_modules",
33:     "tree-sitter-twig",
34:     "tree-sitter-twig.wasm"
35:   )
36: 
37:   if not vim.uv.fs_stat(wasm_source) then
38:     return nil
39:   end
40: 
41:   local extension_path = vim.fs.joinpath(vim.fn.stdpath("cache"), "twig-language-server")
42:   local out_dir = vim.fs....

+
+local function copilot_sign_in(bufnr, client)
+  client:request("signIn", vim.empty_dict(), function(err, result)
+    if err then
+      vim.notify(err.message, vim.log.levels.ERROR)
+      return
+    end
+
+    if result.command then
+      local code = result.userCode
+      vim.fn.setreg("+", code)
+      vim.fn.setreg("*", code)
+
+      local continue = vim.fn.confirm(
+        "Copied your one-time code to clipboard.\nOpen the browser to complete the sign-in process?",
+        "&Yes\n&No"
+      )
+
+      if continue == 1 then
+        client:exec_cmd(result.command, { bufnr = bufnr }, function(cmd_err, cmd_result)
+          if cmd_err then
+            vim.notify(cmd_err.message, vim.log.levels.ERROR)
+            return
+          end
+
+          if cmd_result.status == "OK" then
+            vim.notify("Signed in as " .. cmd_result.user .. ".")
+          end
+        end)
+      end
+    end
+
+    if result.status == "PromptUserDeviceFlow" then
+      vim.notify("Enter your one-time code " .. result.userCode .. " in " .. result.verificationUri)
+    elseif result.status == "AlreadySignedIn" then
+      vim.notify("Already signed in as " .. result.user .. ".")
+    end
+  end)
+end
+

+  client:request("signOut", vim.empty_dict(), function(err, result)
+    if err then

policy-templates | Policy Templates for Firefox                  

# [policy-templates](https://mozilla.github.io/policy-templates/)

> \[!WARNING\] Documentation for policy behavior and syntax is being migrated to the [Firefox administrator reference](https://firefox-admin-docs.mozilla.org/).

Firefox policies can be specified using the [Group Policy templates on Windows](https://github.com/mozilla/policy-templates/tree/master/windows), [Intune on Windows](https://support.mozilla.org/kb/managing-firefox-intune), [configuration profiles on macOS](https://github.com/mozilla/policy-templates/tree/master/mac), or by creating a file called `policies.json`. On Windows, create a directory called `distribution` where the EXE is located and place the file there. On Mac, the file goes into `Firefox.app/Contents/Resources/distribution`. On Linux, the file goes into `firefox/distribution`, where `firefox` is the installation directory for firefox, which varies by distribution or you can specify system-wide policy by placing the file in `/etc/firefox/policies`.

Unfortunately, JSON files do not support comments, but you can add extra entries to the JSON to use as comments. You will see an error in about:policies, but the policies will still work properly. For example:

```
{
  "policies": {
    "Authentication": {
      "SPNEGO": ["mydomain.com", "https://myotherdomain.com"]
    }
    "Authentication_Comment": "These domains are required for us"
  }
}
```

Note: The `policies.json` must use the UTF-8 encoding.

Policy Name

Description

**[`3rdparty`](#3rdparty)**

Set policies that WebExtensions can access via chrome.storage.managed.

**[`AIControls`](#aicontrols)**

Configure AI controls.

**[`AllowedDomainsForApps`](#alloweddomainsforapps)**

Define domains allowed to access Google Workspace.

**[`AllowFileSelectionDialogs`](#allowfileselectiondialogs)**

Allow file selection dialogs.

**[`AppAutoUpdate`](#appautoupdate)**

Enable or disable automatic application update.

**[`AppUpdatePin`](#appupdatepin)**

Prevent Firefox from being updated beyond the specified version.

**[`AppUpdateURL`](#appupdateurl)**

Change the URL for application update.

**[`Authentication`](#authentication)**

**[`AutofillAddressEnabled`](#autofilladdressenabled)**

**[`AutoLaunchProtocolsFromOrigins`](#autolaunchprotocolsfromorigins)**

**[`BlockAboutAddons`](#blockaboutaddons)**

### AIControls

Configure AI controls. For more information, see [Block generative AI features with Firefox AI controls](https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/firefox-ai-controls) on support.mozilla.org.

Each key controls the availability of a specific AI feature. The following AI feature keys are available:

-   `Default`: Controls the default state for AI features listed below, unless they are explicitly configured in the policy.
-   `Translations`: Controls AI-powered page translations.
-   `PDFAltText`: Controls AI-generated alt text for images in PDF documents.
-   `SmartTabGroups`: Controls AI-powered tab grouping suggestions.
-   `LinkPreviewKeyPoints`: Controls AI-generated key point summaries shown in link previews.
-   `SidebarChatbot`: Controls the AI chatbot panel in the Firefox sidebar.
-   `SmartWindow`: Controls AI-powered window arrangement features. (Firefox 150)

All keys accept the following sub-keys:

-   `Value`:
    -   `available` makes the feature accessible to users and it can be enabled or disabled.
    -   `blocked` disables the feature and users won’t see it. For on-device AI, any models already downloaded are removed.
-   `Locked`: if `true`, the user cannot change the setting.

**Compatibility:** Firefox 149.0.2 (SmartWindow: Firefox 150)  
**CCK2 Equivalent:** N/A  
**Preferences Affected:** `browser.ml.chat.enabled`, `browser.ml.chat.page`, `browser.ai.control.sidebarChatbot`, `browser.translations.enable`, `browser.ai.control.translations`, `pdfjs.enableAltText`, `browser.ai.control.pdfjsAltText`, `browser.ml.linkPreview.enabled`, `browser.ai.control.linkPreviewKeyPoints`, `browser.tabs.groups.smart.userEnabled`, `browser.ai.control.smartTabGroups`, `browser.ai.control.smartWindow`

### AllowedDomainsForApps

Define domains allowed to access Google Workspace.

This policy is based on the [Chrome policy](https://chromeenterprise.google/policies/#AllowedDomainsForApps) of the same name.

If this policy is enabled, users can only access Google Workspace using accounts from the specified domains. If you want to allow Gmail, you can add `consumer_accounts` to the list.

**Compatibility:** Firefox 89, Firefox ESR 78.11  
**CCK2 Equivalent:** N/A

### AppAutoUpdate

Enable or disable **automatic** application update.

If set to true, application updates are installed without user approval within Firefox. The operating system might still require approval.

If set to false, application updates are downloaded but the user can choose when to install the update.

If you have disabled updates via `DisableAppUpdate`, this policy has no effect.

**Compatibility:** Firefox 75, Firefox ESR 68.7  
**CCK2 Equivalent:** N/A  
**Preferences Affected:** `app.update.auto`

### AppUpdatePin

Prevent Firefox from being updated beyond the specified version.

You can specify the version as `xx.` and Firefox will be updated with all minor versions, but will not be updated beyond the major version.

You can also specify the version as `xx.xx.` and Firefox will be updated with all patch versions, but will not be updated beyond the minor version.

Note: The value MUST end in a dot(.).

You should specify a version that exists or is guaranteed to exist. If you specify a version that doesn’t end up existing, Firefox will update beyond that version.

**Compatibility:** Firefox 102,  
**CCK2 Equivalent:** N/A  
**Preferences Affected:** N/A

### AutoLaunchProtocolsFromOrigins

Define a list of external protocols that can be used from listed origins without prompting the user. The origin is the scheme plus the hostname.

The syntax of this policy is exactly the same as the [Chrome AutoLaunchProtocolsFromOrigins policy](https://chromeenterprise.google/policies/#AutoLaunchProtocolsFromOrigins) except that you can only use valid origins (not just hostnames). This also means that you cannot specify an asterisk for all origins.

The schema is:

```
{
 "items": {
  "properties": {
   "allowed_origins": {
    "items": {
     "type": "string"
    },
    "type": "array"
   },
   "protocol": {
    "type": "string"
   }
  },
  "required": [
   "protocol",
   "allowed_origins"
  ],
  "type": "object"
 },
 "type": "array"
}
```

**Compatibility:** Firefox 90, Firefox ESR 78.12  
**CCK2 Equivalent:** N/A  
**Preferences Affected:** N/A

#### Windows (GPO)

Software\\Policies\\Mozilla\\Firefox\\AutoLaunchProtocolsFromOrigins (REG\_MULTI\_SZ) =

```
[
  {
    "protocol": "zoommtg",
    "allowed_origins": [
      "https://somesite.zoom.us"
    ]
  }
]
```

#### Windows (Intune)

OMA-URI:

```
./Device/Vendor/MSFT/Policy/Config/Firefox~Policy~firefox/AutoLaunchProtocolsFromOrigins
```

Value (string):

```

<data id="JSON" value='
[
  {
    "protocol": "zoommtg",
    "allowed_origins": [
      "https://somesite.zoom.us"
    ]
  }
]'/>
```

If you are using custom ADMX and ADML administrative templates in Intune, you can use this OMA-URI instead to workaround the limit on the length of strings. Put all of your JSON on one line.

OMA-URI:

```
./Device/Vendor/MSFT/Policy/Config/Firefox~Policy~firefox/AutoLaunchProtocolsFromOriginsOneLine
```

Value (string):

```
<enabled/>
<data id="JSONOneLine" value='[]'/>
```

</dict>

### BackgroundAppUpdate

Enable or disable **automatic** application update **in the background**, when the application is not running.

If set to true, application updates may be installed (without user approval) in the background, even when the application is not running. The operating system might still require approval.

If set to false, the application will not try to install updates when the application is not running.

If you have disabled updates via `DisableAppUpdate` or disabled automatic updates via `AppAutoUpdate`, this policy has no effect.

If you are having trouble getting the background task to run, verify your configuration with the [“Requirements to run” section in this support document](https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/enable-background-updates-firefox-windows).

**Compatibility:** Firefox 90 (Windows only)  
**CCK2 Equivalent:** N/A  
**Preferences Affected:** `app.update.background.enabled`
