[](#blurclean-firefox-theme)

[![](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/49fbde4ff3e07a2a42e59f5333349edba717ab5d8b093fd5301e95ed39ea96d4/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f62616467652f46697265666f785f42726f777365722d4646373133393f7374796c653d666f722d7468652d6261646765266c6f676f3d46697265666f782d42726f77736572266c6f676f436f6c6f723d7768697465)](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/49fbde4ff3e07a2a42e59f5333349edba717ab5d8b093fd5301e95ed39ea96d4/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f62616467652f46697265666f785f42726f777365722d4646373133393f7374796c653d666f722d7468652d6261646765266c6f676f3d46697265666f782d42726f77736572266c6f676f436f6c6f723d7768697465) [![](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/8034744803c27c8aaab4d20b74379109552d328526f309b09269da959b49fe0a/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f62616467652f435353332d3135373242363f7374796c653d666f722d7468652d6261646765266c6f676f3d63737333266c6f676f436f6c6f723d7768697465)](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/8034744803c27c8aaab4d20b74379109552d328526f309b09269da959b49fe0a/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f62616467652f435353332d3135373242363f7374796c653d666f722d7468652d6261646765266c6f676f3d63737333266c6f676f436f6c6f723d7768697465)

[![Example look with theme](/Filip-Sutkowy/blurclean-firefox-theme/raw/master/other/browser.png)](/Filip-Sutkowy/blurclean-firefox-theme/blob/master/other/browser.png)  
*Example look of the browser*

## About

[](#about)

-   supports both dark and light theme
-   tested on Manjaro KDE and Windows 10
-   by default only brings back icons in menus and makes urlbar transparent
-   theme is created using many files imported in userCrome, if you do not want some features you can simply comment out lines with `@import`
-   transparency on popups is limited to urlbar, because you can't get blur on them and it looks bad without it
-   window transparencies make problems on non GNU/Linux systems, but this is not problem by default

## Installation

[](#installation)

1.  Open `about:config` and set this preferences to **'true'**

-   **`toolkit.legacyUserProfileCustomizations.stylesheets`**
-   **`svg.context-properties.content.enabled`**
-   **`layers.acceleration.force-enabled`**
-   **`gfx.webrender.all`**
-   **`gfx.webrender.enabled`**
-   **`layout.css.backdrop-filter.enabled`**

2.  Go to `about:profiles` and find your `Root Directory` and enter there.
3.  Next step you can solve with two ways:

-   create new directory `chrome` and copy there repo content
-   open terminal, when you are in `Root Directory` use `git clone https://github.com/Filip-Sutkowy/blurclean-firefox-theme chrome`

## Optional steps

[](#optional-steps)

-   Install TreeStyleTab addon and paste content of `tst_theme.css` in Tree Style Tab / Preferences / Advanced / Extra style.
-   Uncomment optional TST styles in userChrome
-   Uncomment optional transparencies for window (only for GNU/Linux)
-   move `Bookmarks toolbar items` next to url bar to save some vertical space

## Blur

[](#blur)

First, make sure that you have enabled optional styles in userChrome. Then you have to enable blur in compositor, where to find this option is based on your desktop environment, here is how to enable it with KDE:

-   Enable blur in System Settings / Workspace / Workspace Behavior / Deskop Effects
-   Download and enable **Force Blur** script in System Settings / Workspace / Window Management / KWin Scripts. Relogin to enable script configuration. Now add firefox in script config.
-   **Force Blur** need this two command to start working (maybe it's already fixed, check comments [here](https://store.kde.org/p/1294604/))
    -   `mkdir -p ~/.local/share/kservices5/`
    -   `cp ~/.local/share/kwin/scripts/forceblur/metadata.desktop ~/.local/share/kservices5/forceblur.desktop`
-   Now should working

## Reccomended Addons

[](#reccomended-addons)

These addons make this theme better

-   [TST Colored Tabs](https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/tst-colored-tabs) - You can use it instead of colouring in `tst_theme.css`, I don't use this addon because you can't get tranparency on tabs.
-   [Dark Reader](https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/darkreader) | [Dark Background and Light Text](https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/dark-background-light-text/) | [Darker Background Brighter Font](https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/darker-bg-brighter-font) or other addons which makes every page **DARK**
-   [Tabliss](https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/tabliss/) | [Momentum](https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/momentumdash) | [Speed Dial](https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/fvd-speed-dial) or other new page extension

## Uninstalling

[](#uninstalling)

Just remove `chrome` folder, you can also reset changed config options, but this is not nessesary.

## About

Clean, transparent firefox theme

<path>/Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: #!/usr/bin/env bash
2: # Apply dotfiles-managed Firefox policy and default-profile preferences on macOS.
3: 
4: set -euo pipefail
5: 
6: SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
7: FIREFOX_APP="${FIREFOX_APP:-/Applications/Firefox.app}"
8: FIREFOX_ROOT="${FIREFOX_ROOT:-$HOME/Library/Application Support/Firefox}"
9: POLICY_SOURCE="${FIREFOX_POLICY_SOURCE:-$SCRIPT_DIR/firefox/policies.json}"
10: USER_JS_SOURCE="${FIREFOX_USER_JS_SOURCE:-$SCRIPT_DIR/firefox/user.js}"
11: USER_CHROME_SOURCE="${FIREFOX_USER_CHROME_SOURCE:-$SCRIPT_DIR/firefox/chrome/userChrome.css}"
12: CAPTURE_PREFS_HELPER="$SCRIPT_DIR/firefox-capture-prefs.py"
13: 
14: BLUE='\033[0;34m'
15: GREEN='\033[0;32m'
16: YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
17: NC='\033[0m'
18: 
19: log_info() {
20:     echo -e "${BLUE}[INFO]${NC} $1"
21: }
22: 
23: log_success() {
24:     echo -e "${GREEN}[SUCCESS]${NC} $1"
25: }
26: 
27: log_warning() {
28:     echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARNING]${NC} $1"
29: }
30: 
31: backup_existing_file() {
32:     local target_path="$1"
33:     local backup_path=""
34: 
35:     backup_path="${target_path}.backup.$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)"
36: 
37:     if cp "$target_path" "$backup_path"; then
38:         log_info "Backed up existing $(basename "$target_path") to $backup_path"
39:         return 0
40:     fi
41: 
42:     log_warning "Could not back up existing $(basename "$target_path")"
43:     return 1
44: }
45: 
46: copy_managed_file() {
47:     local source_path="$1"
48:     local target_path="$2"
49:     local label="$3"
50: 
51:     if [[ ! -f "$source_path" ]]; then
52:         log_warning "$label source missing: $source_path"
53:         return 0
54:     fi
55: 
56:     if [[ -f "$target_path" ]] && cmp -s "$source_path" "$target_path"; then
57:         log_success "$label already current"
58:         return 0
59:     fi
60: 
61:     if [[ -e "$target_path" && ! -f "$target_path" && ! -L "$target_path" ]]; then
62:         log_warning "$label target exists but is not a file: $target_path"
63:         return 1
64:     fi
65: 
66:     if ! mkdir -p "$(dirname "$target_path")" 2>/dev/null; then
67:         log_warning "Could not create $(dirname "$target_path")"
68:         return 1
69:     fi
70: 
71:     if [[ -f "$target_path" || -L "$target_path" ]]; then
72:         if ! backup_existing_file "$target_path"; then
73:             return 1
74:         fi
75: 
76:         if ! rm -f "$target_path"; then
77:             log_warning "Could not replace existing $label at $target_path"
78:             return 1
79:         fi
80:     fi
81: 
82:     if cp "$source_path" "$target_path"; then
83:         chmod 0644 "$target_path" 2>/dev/null || true
84:         log_success "Installed $label"
85:         return 0
86:     fi
87: 
88:     log_warning "Could not install $label to $target_path"
89:     return 1
90: }
91: 
92: resolve_profile_path() {
93:     local profile_ref="$1"
94: 
95:     if [[ "$profile_ref" == /* ]]; then
96:         printf '%s\n' "$profile_ref"
97:     else
98:         printf '%s/%s\n' "$FIREFOX_ROOT" "$profile_ref"
99:     fi
100: }
101: 
102: find_default_profile() {
103:     local profiles_ini="$FIREFOX_ROOT/profiles.ini"
104:     local section=""
105:     local path=""
106:     local is_relative="1"
107:     local is_default="0"
108:     local install_default=""
109:     local profile_default=""
110:     local profile_default_relative="1"
111:     local first_profile=""
112:     local first_profile_relative="1"
113: 
114:     [[ -f "$profiles_ini" ]] || return 1
115: 
116:     commit_profile_section() {
117:         if [[ "$section" != Profile* || -z "$path" ]]; then
118:             return 0
119:         fi
120: 
121:         if [[ -z "$first_profile" ]]; then
122:             first_profile="$path"
123:             first_profile_relative="$is_relative"
124:         fi
125: 
126:         if [[ "$is_default" == "1" ]]; then
127:             profile_default="$path"
128:             profile_default_relative="$is_relative"
129:         fi
130:     }
131: 
132:     while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
133:         line="${line%$'\r'}"
134: 
135:         case "$line" in
136:         '' | \#* | \;*)
137:             continue
138:             ;;
139:         \[*\])
140:             commit_profile_section
141:             section="${line#[}"
142:             section="${section%]}"
143:             path=""
144:             is_relative="1"
145:             is_default="0"
146:             continue
147:             ;;
148:         esac
149: 
150:         case "$section" in
151:         Install*)
152:             case "$line" in
153:             Default=*) install_default="${line#Default=}" ;;
154:             esac
155:             ;;
156:         Profile*)
157:             case "$line" in
158:             Path=*) path="${line#Path=}" ;;
159:             IsRelative=*) is_relative="${line#IsRelative=}" ;;
160:             Default=1) is_default="1" ;;
161:             esac
162:             ;;
163:         esac
164:     done <"$profiles_ini"
165: 
166:     commit_profile_section
167: 
168:     if [[ -n "$install_default" ]]; then
169:         resolve_profile_path "$install_default"
170:         return 0
171:     fi
172: 
173:     if [[ -n "$profile_default" ]]; then
174:         if [[ "$profile_default_relative" == "1" ]]; then
175:             resolve_profile_path "$profile_default"
176:         else
177:             printf '%s\n' "$profile_default"
178:         fi
179:         return 0
180:     fi
181: 
182:     if [[ -n "$first_profile" ]]; then
183:         if [[ "$first_profile_relative" == "1" ]]; then
184:             resolve_profile_path "$first_profile"
185:         else
186:             printf '%s\n' "$first_profile"
187:         fi
188:         return 0
189:     fi
190: 
191:     return 1
192: }
193: 
194: install_policy() {
195:     local policy_target="$FIREFOX_APP/Contents/Resources/distribution/policies.json"
196: 
197:     if [[ ! -d "$FIREFOX_APP" ]]; then
198:         log_warning "Firefox.app not found at $FIREFOX_APP; skipping enterprise policy"
199:         return 0
200:     fi
201: 
202:     if copy_managed_file "$POLICY_SOURCE" "$policy_target" "Firefox policies.json"; then
203:         return 0
204:     fi
205: 
206:     log_warning "Manual policy install: sudo mkdir -p \"$(dirname "$policy_target")\" && sudo cp \"$POLICY_SOURCE\" \"$policy_target\""
207:     return 1
208: }
209: 
210: install_user_js() {
211:     local profile_path=""
212: 
213:     if ! profile_path="$(find_default_profile)"; then
214:         log_warning "No Firefox profile found; run Firefox once, then rerun setup to install user.js"
215:         return 0
216:     fi
217: 
218:     if [[ ! -d "$profile_path" ]]; then
219:         log_warning "Default Firefox profile directory does not exist: $profile_path"
220:         return 0
221:     fi
222: 
223:     copy_managed_file "$USER_JS_SOURCE" "$profile_path/user.js" "Firefox default-profile user.js" || true
224: }
225: 
226: install_user_chrome() {
227:     local profile_path=""
228: 
229:     if ! profile_path="$(find_default_profile)"; then
230:         log_warning "No Firefox profile found; run Firefox once, then rerun setup to install userChrome.css"
231:         return 0
232:     fi
233: 
234:     if [[ ! -d "$profile_path" ]]; then
235:         log_warning "Default Firefox profile directory does not exist: $profile_path"
236:         return 0
237:     fi
238: 
239:     copy_managed_file "$USER_CHROME_SOURCE" "$profile_path/chrome/userChrome.css" "Firefox Sidebery userChrome.css" || true
240: }
241: 
242: capture_current_prefs() {
243:     if ! command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
244:         log_warning "python3 is required to capture Firefox prefs"
245:         return 1
246:     fi
247: 
248:     python3 "$CAPTURE_PREFS_HELPER" --firefox-root "$FIREFOX_ROOT" --user-js "$USER_JS_SOURCE" "$@"
249: }
250: 
251: self_test() {
252:     local tmp_dir=""
253:     local profile_dir=""
254:     local old_firefox_app=""
255:     local old_firefox_root=""
256:     local result=0
257: 
258:     old_firefox_app="$FIREFOX_APP"
259:     old_firefox_root="$FIREFOX_ROOT"
260:

| `codex/` | Codex CLI integration and account management |
| `otel/` | OpenTelemetry LGTM stack (docker-compose, Grafana dashboards) |
| `obsidian/` | Obsidian vault management scripts |
| `openclaw/` | OpenClaw AI platform config |

### DevOps & Containers
| Directory | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| `docker/` | Dockerfile and container testing |
| `devcontainer/` | Devcontainer credential export, auto-login |
| `kubernetes/` | K8s helper scripts |
| `manifests/` | K8s manifest files (with README) |
| `linux/` | Linux-specific setup scripts |
| `windows/` | Windows/WSL setup |
| `windows-vm/` | Windows VM provisioning |

### Domain-Specific
| Directory | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| `youtube/` | YouTube transcript fetcher (yt-transcript.py) |
| `anki/` | Anki flashcard generation |
| `cv/` | CV compilation and LaTeX tooling |
| `aws/` | AWS helper scripts |
| `tmux/` | tmux watcher, session management |
| `pihole/` | Pi-hole DNS management |
| `ticket-queue/` | Ticket queue management |
| `tools/` | Standalone tool scripts |

## Conventions

- Bash scripts: `set -euo pipefail`, quote all variables, snake_case functions
- File naming: kebab-case (e.g., `setup-mobile-coding.sh`)
- Shared code: import from `lib/` directory
- Never use `((var++))` with `set -e` — use `var=$((var + 1))` instead

</system-reminder>

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Apply dotfiles-managed Firefox policy and default-profile preferences on macOS.

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
FIREFOX_APP="${FIREFOX_APP:-/Applications/Firefox.app}"
FIREFOX_ROOT="${FIREFOX_ROOT:-$HOME/Library/Application Support/Firefox}"
POLICY_SOURCE="${FIREFOX_POLICY_SOURCE:-$SCRIPT_DIR/firefox/policies.json}"
USER_JS_SOURCE="${FIREFOX_USER_JS_SOURCE:-$SCRIPT_DIR/firefox/user.js}"
USER_CHROME_SOURCE="${FIREFOX_USER_CHROME_SOURCE:-$SCRIPT_DIR/firefox/chrome/userChrome.css}"
CAPTURE_PREFS_HELPER="$SCRIPT_DIR/firefox-capture-prefs.py"

BLUE='\033[0;34m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
NC='\033[0m'

log_info() {
    echo -e "${BLUE}[INFO]${NC} $1"

run_test ".mcp.json exists" "[ -f '$mcp_config' ]"
    if [ -f "$mcp_config" ]; then
        run_test ".mcp.json is valid JSON" "python3 -c \"import json; json.load(open('$mcp_config'))\""
    fi

    if [ -f "$desktop_config" ] && [ -f "$mcp_config" ]; then
        run_test "Desktop MCP matches .mcp.json" "python3 -c \"import json; repo=json.load(open('$mcp_config')).get('mcpServers', {}); desk=json.load(open('$desktop_config')).get('mcpServers', {}); assert desk == repo\""
        run_test ".mcp.json MCP servers use bunx" "python3 -c \"import json; servers=json.load(open('$mcp_config')).get('mcpServers', {}); assert servers and all(server.get('command') == 'bunx' for server in servers.values())\""
        run_test "setup.sh registers .mcp.json stdio servers" "python3 -c \"import json, pathlib, re; servers=set(json.load(open('$mcp_config')).get('mcpServers', {})); setup=pathlib.Path('$DOTFILES_ROOT/scripts/setup.sh').read_text(); configured=set(re.findall(r'claude mcp add --scope user ([a-z0-9-]+) ', setup)); assert servers <= configured\""
    fi

    run_test "setup.sh uses bunx for shared stdio MCP servers" "python3 -c \"import pathlib; text=pathlib.Path('$DOTFILES_ROOT/scripts/setup.sh').read_text(); required=['context7 bunx', 'steampipe bunx', 'playwright bunx', 'drawio bunx']; assert all(item in text for item in required)\""
    run_test "setup.sh keeps deepwiki SSE exception" "grep -q 'claude mcp add --scope user --transport sse deepwiki https://mcp.deepwiki.com/sse' '$DOTFILES_ROOT/scripts/setup.sh'"

    # deniedMcpServers: blocks unused claude.ai managed integrations to reclaim KV-cache tokens
    local settings="$DOTFILES_ROOT/.claude/settings.json"
    if [ -f "$settings" ]; then
        run_test "settings.json declares deniedMcpServers" "python3 -c \"import json; s=json.load(open('$settings')); assert isinstance(s.get('deniedMcpServers'), list) and len(s['deniedMcpServers']) > 0\""
        run_test "deniedMcpServers includes unused integrations" "python3 -c \"import json; denied=set(json.load(open('$settings')).get('deniedMcpServers', [])); required={'claude_ai_Notion','claude_ai_Linear','claude_ai_Invideo'}; assert required <= denied, f'missing: {required - denied}'\""
        run_test "deniedMcpServers keeps used integrations unblocked" "python3 -c \"import json; denied=set(json.load(open('$settings')).get('deniedMcpServers', [])); used={'claude_ai_Atlassian','claude_ai_Gmail','claude_ai_Google_Calendar','claude_ai_Google_Drive'}; assert not (used & denied), f'blocks used: {used & denied}'\""

<path>/Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.granted/README.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # Granted Configuration Guide
2: 
3: This directory contains configuration for [Granted](https://granted.dev), an AWS credential management tool with autocomplete, browser integration, and Firefox container colors/icons.
4: 
5: ## What You Should See
6: 
7: ### Terminal Autocomplete
8: 
9: When you type `assume` followed by space and Tab:
10: 
11: ```bash
12: assume <TAB>
13: ```
14: 
15: Expected: AWS profile names from `~/.aws/config`.
16: 
17: Not expected: Granted command options or local file names.
18: 
19: ### Firefox Browser Integration
20: 
21: When you open an AWS console through Granted:
22: 
23: - Firefox opens instead of Chrome or Safari.
24: - Granted opens the console in a Firefox container tab.
25: - Container colors/icons come from `.granted/firefox-profiles`.
26: 
27: Examples:
28: 
29: ```text
30: production=red:briefcase
31: development=green:tree
32: staging=yellow:cart
33: security=purple:fingerprint
34: ```
35: 
36: ## Setup
37: 
38: ### Automatic Setup
39: 
40: The dotfiles setup script handles the reproducible pieces:
41: 
42: ```bash
43: ./scripts/setup.sh
44: ```
45: 
46: Setup does the following:
47: 
48: - Installs Firefox from `homebrew/Brewfile` and `phase_9_fonts_and_apps`.
49: - Uses stow to sync `.granted/config` and `.granted/firefox-profiles` into `~/.granted`.
50: - Applies `scripts/setup/firefox/policies.json` to the Firefox app bundle when writable.
51: - Installs `scripts/setup/firefox/user.js` into the default local Firefox profile only.
52: - Installs `scripts/setup/firefox/chrome/userChrome.css` into the default local profile to hide native horizontal tabs when using Sidebery.
53: 
54: The `user.js` syncs selected `about:config` preferences without taking ownership of cookies, history, sessions, logins, cache, or the full Firefox profile.
55: 
56: To refresh `user.js` from the current Firefox default profile:
57: 
58: ```bash
59: ~/dotfiles/scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh --capture-current-prefs
60: ```
61: 
62: The capture helper reads `prefs.js`, keeps a conservative allowlist of portable preferences, and excludes profile/session/device-specific values such as local paths, Sync account state, telemetry IDs, cache state, downloads, and extension runtime UUIDs.
63: 
64: ### Manual Fallback
65: 
66: If automatic Firefox policy installation is skipped because the app bundle is not writable:
67: 
68: ```bash
69: sudo mkdir -p "/Applications/Firefox.app/Contents/Resources/distribution"
70: sudo cp ~/dotfiles/scripts/setup/firefox/policies.json "/Applications/Firefox.app/Contents/Resources/distribution/policies.json"
71: ```
72: 
73: If Firefox has not created a profile yet, launch Firefox once and then rerun:
74: 
75: ```bash
76: ~/dotfiles/scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh
77: ```
78: 
79: If the Granted extension is still missing, install it from AMO:
80: 
81: ```text
82: https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/granted/
83: ```
84: 
85: Check active Firefox enterprise policies at `about:policies`.
86: 
87: ## Configuration Files
88: 
89: ### `.granted/config`
90: 
91: Main Granted configuration:
92: 
93: - Sets Firefox as the default Granted browser.
94: - Points Granted at `/Applications/Firefox.app/Contents/MacOS/firefox`.
95: - Uses the macOS keychain backend.
96: 
97: ### `.granted/firefox-profiles`
98: 
99: Maps AWS profile names to Firefox container colors and icons:
100: 
101: ```text
102: production=red:briefcase
103: prod=red:briefcase
104: development=green:tree
105: dev=green:tree
106: management=blue:briefcase
107: security=purple:fingerprint
108: ```
109: 
110: ### `scripts/setup/firefox/policies.json`
111: 
112: Machine-level Firefox enterprise policies:
113: 
114: - Force-installs the Granted Firefox extension.
115: - Disables Firefox studies, telemetry, Pocket, default-browser prompts, and first-run/post-update pages.
116: 
117: ### `scripts/setup/firefox/user.js`
118: 
119: Default-profile `about:config` preferences:
120: 
121: - Enables Firefox containers and the container UI.
122: - Mirrors lightweight telemetry/new-tab defaults.
123: - Includes a generated block of safe preferences captured from the current local Firefox default profile.
124: - Does not manage profile databases such as `cookies.sqlite`, `places.sqlite`, `logins.json`, `key4.db`, or session restore files.
125: 
126: ### `scripts/setup/firefox/chrome/userChrome.css`
127: 
128: Default-profile Firefox chrome CSS:
129: 
130: - Hides Firefox's native horizontal tab strip so Sidebery is the only tab UI.
131: - Keeps macOS/window-control support from the upstream Firefox CSS hack.
132: - Requires `toolkit.legacyUserProfileCustomizations.stylesheets=true`, which is set in `user.js`.
133: - Requires a full Firefox restart after installation.
134: 
135: ## Available Colors And Icons
136: 
137: Colors: blue, turquoise, green, yellow, orange, red, pink, purple.
138: 
139: Icons: fingerprint, briefcase, dollar, cart, circle, gift, vacation, food, fruit, pet, tree, chill.
140: 
141: ## Usage Examples
142: 
143: ```bash
144: # Basic profile switching
145: assume production
146: 
147: # Open AWS console in Firefox with the configured container
148: assume -c production
149: 
150: # List available profiles
151: granted profiles list
152: 
153: # Configure profile colors in the local Granted config
154: ~/dotfiles/scripts/setup/granted-setup.sh set-profile-color myprofile blue tree
155: 
156: # Test local Granted configuration
157: ~/dotfiles/scripts/setup/granted-setup.sh test-config
158: ```
159: 
160: ## Troubleshooting
161: 
162: ### Autocomplete Shows Wrong Items
163: 
164: Problem: `assume <TAB>` shows files or Granted commands instead of AWS profiles.
165: 
166: Solution:
167: 
168: ```bash
169: fish -c "source ~/.config/fish/completions/granted.fish"
170: ```
171: 
172: ### No Colors Or Icons In Firefox
173: 
174: Problem: Firefox tabs are plain, with no Granted colors or icons.
175: 
176: Solution:
177: 
178: 1. Check `about:policies` for the Granted extension policy.
179: 2. Confirm the Granted Firefox addon is installed: `about:addons`.
180: 3. Ensure Firefox is set as Granted's browser: `granted browser set firefox`.
181: 4. Check profile mappings in `~/.granted/firefox-profiles`.
182: 
183: ### About:Config Settings Did Not Apply
184: 
185: Problem: Firefox preferences from dotfiles are missing.
186: 
187: Solution:
188: 
189: 1. Quit Firefox completely.
190: 2. Run `~/dotfiles/scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh`.
191: 3. Reopen Firefox.
192: 4. Check that the default profile has `user.js` under `~/Library/Application Support/Firefox/Profiles/<profile>/user.js`.
193: 
194: ### Native Top Tabs Still Show With Sidebery
195: 
196: Problem: Sidebery is open, but Firefox still shows the native horizontal tab bar at the top.
197: 
198: Solution:
199: 
200: 1. Quit Firefox completely.
201: 2. Run `~/dotfiles/scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh`.
202: 3. Reopen Firefox.
203: 4. Check that the default profile has `chrome/userChrome.css` under `~/Library/Application Support/Firefox/Profiles/<profile>/chrome/userChrome.css`.
204: 5. Confirm `toolkit.legacyUserProfileCustomizations.stylesheets` is `true` in `about:config`.
205: 
206: ### Wrong Browser Opens
207: 
208: Problem: Chrome or Safari opens instead of Firefox.
209: 
210: Solution:
211: 
212: ```bash
213: granted browser set firefox
214: ```
215: 
216: ### Profile Not Found
217: 
218: Problem: Granted reports profile-not-found errors.
219: 
220: Solution:
221: 
222: 1. Check AWS configuration: `aws configure list-profiles`.
223: 2. Verify AWS config exists: `ls ~/.aws/config`.
224: 3. Ensure profile names match `.granted/firefox-profiles` exactly.
225: 
226: ## Helper Scripts
227: 
228: Use `~/dotfiles/scripts/setup/granted-setup.sh` for Granted config management:
229: 
230: - `setup` installs local Granted config files by copying them from dotfiles.
231: - `set-profile-color PROFILE COLOR ICON` configures colors/icons.
232: - `list-profiles` shows configured Firefox profile mappings.
233: - `test-config` validates local Granted setup.
234: - `list-colors` shows available options.
235: 
236: Use `~/dotfiles/scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh` for Firefox policy, `user.js`, and Sidebery `userChrome.css` setup. Use `~/dotfiles/scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh --capture-current-prefs` to refresh the dotfiles-managed `user.js` from the current Firefox default profile.
237: 
238: ## Links
239: 
240: - Documentation: https://docs.commonfate.io/granted/
241: - Firefox Addon: https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/granted/
242: - GitHub: https://github.com/common-fate/granted

(End of file - total 242 lines)
</content>

# [policy-templates](https://mozilla.github.io/policy-templates/)

> \[!WARNING\] Documentation for policy behavior and syntax is being migrated to the [Firefox administrator reference](https://firefox-admin-docs.mozilla.org/).

Firefox policies can be specified using the [Group Policy templates on Windows](https://github.com/mozilla/policy-templates/tree/master/windows), [Intune on Windows](https://support.mozilla.org/kb/managing-firefox-intune), [configuration profiles on macOS](https://github.com/mozilla/policy-templates/tree/master/mac), or by creating a file called `policies.json`. On Windows, create a directory called `distribution` where the EXE is located and place the file there. On Mac, the file goes into `Firefox.app/Contents/Resources/distribution`. On Linux, the file goes into `firefox/distribution`, where `firefox` is the installation directory for firefox, which varies by distribution or you can specify system-wide policy by placing the file in `/etc/firefox/policies`.

Unfortunately, JSON files do not support comments, but you can add extra entries to the JSON to use as comments. You will see an error in about:policies, but the policies will still work properly. For example:

```
{
  "policies": {
    "Authentication": {
      "SPNEGO": ["mydomain.com", "https://myotherdomain.com"]
    }
    "Authentication_Comment": "These domains are required for us"
  }
}
```

Note: The `policies.json` must use the UTF-8 encoding.

from pathlib import Path
import re
root = Path.home() / 'Library/Application Support/Firefox'
profile = root / 'Profiles/ewb7hoy2.default-release'
prefs = profile / 'prefs.js'
rx = re.compile(r'^user_pref\("((?:\\.|[^"])*)",\s*(.*)\);$')
keys=[]
with prefs.open('r', encoding='utf-8', errors='replace') as f:
    for line in f:
        m=rx.match(line.strip())
        if m:
            keys.append(m.group(1))
print(f'profile={profile}')
print(f'prefs={len(keys)}')
for k in sorted(keys):
    print(k)

+/* Dotfiles-managed Firefox chrome customizations. */
+/* Hides the native horizontal tab strip when Sidebery is the tab UI. */

+

+

+}

@@
 - Does not manage profile databases such as `cookies.sqlite`, `places.sqlite`, `logins.json`, `key4.db`, or session restore files.
+
+### `scripts/setup/firefox/chrome/userChrome.css`
+
+Default-profile Firefox chrome CSS:
+
+- Hides Firefox's native horizontal tab strip so Sidebery is the only tab UI.
+- Keeps macOS/window-control support from the upstream Firefox CSS hack.
+- Requires `toolkit.legacyUserProfileCustomizations.stylesheets=true`, which is set in `user.js`.
+- Requires a full Firefox restart after installation.
@@

1: /* Dotfiles-managed Firefox chrome customizations. */
2: /* Hides the native horizontal tab strip when Sidebery is the tab UI. */
3: /* Adapted from MrOtherGuy/firefox-csshacks hide_tabs_toolbar_v2.css (MPL-2.0). */
4: 
5: @media not -moz-pref("sidebar.verticalTabs") {
6:   #TabsToolbar:not([customizing]) {
7:     visibility: collapse !important;
8:   }
9: 
10:   :root[sizemode="fullscreen"] #nav-bar > .titlebar-buttonbox-container,
11:   :root[customtitlebar] #toolbar-menubar:is([autohide=""], [autohide="true"]) ~ #nav-bar > .titlebar-buttonbox-container {
12:     display: flex !important;
13:   }
14: 
15:   :root[customtitlebar][sizemode="normal"] #toolbar-menubar:is([autohide=""], [autohide="true"]) ~ #nav-bar > .titlebar-spacer,
16:   :root[customtitlebar][sizemode="maximized"] #toolbar-menubar:is([autohide=""], [autohide="true"]) ~ #nav-bar > .titlebar-spacer[type="post-tabs"] {
17:     display: flex !important;
18:   }
19: 
20:   @media (-moz-platform: macos) {
21:     :root[customtitlebar][sizemode="maximized"] #toolbar-menubar:is([autohide=""], [autohide="true"]) ~ #nav-bar > .titlebar-spacer[type="post-tabs"] {
22:       display: none !important;
23:     }

1: // Dotfiles-managed Firefox preferences.
2: // Installed into the default local Firefox profile by scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh.
3: // This file intentionally does not manage cookies, history, sessions, or logins.
4: 
5: // Required for Granted/AWS console isolation via Firefox containers.
6: user_pref("privacy.userContext.enabled", true);
7: user_pref("privacy.userContext.ui.enabled", true);
8: user_pref("privacy.userContext.longPressBehavior", 2);
9: 
10: // Required for userChrome.css customizations, including hidden native tabs for Sidebery.
11: user_pref("toolkit.legacyUserProfileCustomizations.stylesheets", true);
12: 
13: // Keep startup/new-tab noise low without changing browsing data.
14: user_pref("browser.shell.checkDefaultBrowser", false);
15: user_pref("browser.startup.homepage_override.mstone", "ignore");
16: user_pref("browser.bookmarks.restore_default_bookmarks", false);
17: user_pref("browser.newtabpage.activity-stream.feeds.telemetry", false);
18: user_pref("browser.newtabpage.activity-stream.showSponsored", false);

# [policy-templates](https://mozilla.github.io/policy-templates/)

> \[!WARNING\] Documentation for policy behavior and syntax is being migrated to the [Firefox administrator reference](https://firefox-admin-docs.mozilla.org/).

Firefox policies can be specified using the [Group Policy templates on Windows](https://github.com/mozilla/policy-templates/tree/master/windows), [Intune on Windows](https://support.mozilla.org/kb/managing-firefox-intune), [configuration profiles on macOS](https://github.com/mozilla/policy-templates/tree/master/mac), or by creating a file called `policies.json`. On Windows, create a directory called `distribution` where the EXE is located and place the file there. On Mac, the file goes into `Firefox.app/Contents/Resources/distribution`. On Linux, the file goes into `firefox/distribution`, where `firefox` is the installation directory for firefox, which varies by distribution or you can specify system-wide policy by placing the file in `/etc/firefox/policies`.

Unfortunately, JSON files do not support comments, but you can add extra entries to the JSON to use as comments. You will see an error in about:policies, but the policies will still work properly. For example:

```
{
  "policies": {
    "Authentication": {
      "SPNEGO": ["mydomain.com", "https://myotherdomain.com"]
    }
    "Authentication_Comment": "These domains are required for us"
  }
}
```

Note: The `policies.json` must use the UTF-8 encoding.

User then asked: “We use sideberry is it possible to update the config so the tab bars at the top don't show? [Image 1] If so can you apply this config to this device too to fix the issue”. The screenshot showed Firefox with Sidebery open in the left sidebar but Firefox’s native horizontal tab strip still visible across the top.

Plan Mode was NOT active during this implementation segment, so edits and local application were performed.

Research and decision:
- Assistant identified that hiding Firefox’s native horizontal tab strip while using Sidebery requires profile chrome CSS (`chrome/userChrome.css`) and the Firefox pref `toolkit.legacyUserProfileCustomizations.stylesheets=true` in `user.js`.
- Fetched Sidebery wiki page `https://github.com/mbnuqw/sidebery/wiki/Firefox-Styles-Snippets-(via-userChrome.css)` read-only. Key relevant points from the page: Firefox 69+ requires `toolkit.legacyUserProfileCustomizations.stylesheets` enabled; create a `chrome/userChrome.css` file in the profile directory; for completely hiding native tabs, Sidebery wiki points to MrOtherGuy/firefox-csshacks snippets including `hide_tabs_toolbar`, `hide_tabs_toolbar_osx`, and `window_control_placeholder_support`.
- Fetched `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/MrOtherGuy/firefox-csshacks/master/chrome/hide_tabs_toolbar_v2.css` read-only. 1: #!/usr/bin/env bash
2: # Apply dotfiles-managed Firefox policy and default-profile preferences on macOS.
3: 
4: set -euo pipefail
5: 
6: SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
7: FIREFOX_APP="${FIREFOX_APP:-/Applications/Firefox.app}"
8: FIREFOX_ROOT="${FIREFOX_ROOT:-$HOME/Library/Application Support/Firefox}"
9: POLICY_SOURCE="${FIREFOX_POLICY_SOURCE:-$SCRIPT_DIR/firefox/policies.json}"
10: USER_JS_SOURCE="${FIREFOX_USER_JS_SOURCE:-$SCRIPT_DIR/firefox/user.js}"
11: USER_CHROME_SOURCE="${FIREFOX_USER_CHROME_SOURCE:-$SCRIPT_DIR/firefox/chrome/userChrome.css}"
12: CAPTURE_PREFS_HELPER="$SCRIPT_DIR/firefox-capture-prefs.py"
13: 
14: BLUE='\033[0;34m'
15: GREEN='\033[0;32m'
16: YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
17: NC='\033[0m'
18: 
19: log_info() {
20:     echo -e "${BLUE}[INFO]${NC} $1"
21: }
22: 
23: log_success() {
24:     echo -e "${GREEN}[SUCCESS]${NC} $1"
25: }
26: 
27: log_warning() {
28:     echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARNING]${NC} $1"
29: }
30: 
31: backup_existing_file() {
32:     local target_path="$1"
33:     local backup_path=""
34: 
35:     backup_path="${target_path}.backup.$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)"
36: 
37:     if cp "$target_path" "$backup_path"; then
38:         log_info "Backed up existing $(basename "$target_path") to $backup_path"
39:         return 0
40:     fi

1: # Granted Configuration Guide
2: 
3: This directory contains configuration for [Granted](https://granted.dev), an AWS credential management tool with autocomplete, browser integration, and Firefox container colors/icons.
4: 
5: ## What You Should See
6: 
7: ### Terminal Autocomplete
8: 
9: When you type `assume` followed by space and Tab:
10: 
11: ```bash
12: assume <TAB>
13: ```
14: 
15: Expected: AWS profile names from `~/.aws/config`.
16: 
17: Not expected: Granted command options or local file names.
18: 
19: ### Firefox Browser Integration
20: 
21: When you open an AWS console through Granted:
22: 
23: - Firefox opens instead of Chrome or Safari.
24: - Granted opens the console in a Firefox container tab.
25: - Container colors/icons come from `.granted/firefox-profiles`.
26: 
27: Examples:
28: 
29: ```text
30: production=red:briefcase
31: development=green:tree
32: staging=yellow:cart
33: security=purple:fingerprint
34: ```
35: 
36: ## Setup
37: 
38: ### Automatic Setup
39: 
40: The dotfiles setup script handles the reproducible pieces:
41: 
42: ```bash
43: ./scripts/setup.sh
44: ```
45: 
46: Setup does the following:
47: 
48: - Installs Firefox from `homebrew/Brewfile` and `phase_9_fonts_and_apps`.
49: - Uses stow to sync `.granted/config` and `.granted/firefox-profiles` into `~/.granted`.
50: - Applies `scripts/setup/firefox/policies.json` to the Firefox app bundle when writable.
51: - Installs `scripts/setup/fi...

# [policy-templates](https://mozilla.github.io/policy-templates/)

> \[!WARNING\] Documentation for policy behavior and syntax is being migrated to the [Firefox administrator reference](https://firefox-admin-docs.mozilla.org/).

Firefox policies can be specified using the [Group Policy templates on Windows](https://github.com/mozilla/policy-templates/tree/master/windows), [Intune on Windows](https://support.mozilla.org/kb/managing-firefox-intune), [configuration profiles on macOS](https://github.com/mozilla/policy-templates/tree/master/mac), or by creating a file called `policies.json`. On Windows, create a directory called `distribution` where the EXE is located and place the file there. On Mac, the file goes into `Firefox.app/Contents/Resources/distribution`. On Linux, the file goes into `firefox/distribution`, where `firefox` is the installation directory for firefox, which varies by distribution or you can specify system-wide policy by placing the file in `/etc/firefox/policies`.

Unfortunately, JSON files do not support comments, but you can add extra entries to the JSON to use as comments. You will see an error in about:policies, but the policies will still work properly. For example:

```
{
  "policies": {
    "Authentication": {
      "SPNEGO": ["mydomain.com", "https://myotherdomain.com"]
    }
    "Authentication_Comment": "These domains are required for us"
  }
}
```

Note: The `policies.json` must use the UTF-8 encoding.

Policy Name

Description

**[`3rdparty`](#3rdparty)**

Set policies that WebExtensions can access via chrome.storage.managed.

**[`AIControls`](#aicontrols)**

Configure AI controls.

**[`AllowedDomainsForApps`](#alloweddomainsforapps)**

Define domains allowed to access Google Workspace.

**[`AllowFileSelectionDialogs`](#allowfileselectiondialogs)**

Allow file selection dialogs.

+    r'(?P<value>.*)(?P<suffix>\);\s*)$'

state.output:
# blurclean-firefox-theme


Example look of the browser

## About + supports both dark and light theme + tested on Manjaro KDE and Windows 10 + by default only brings back icons in menus and makes urlbar transparent + theme is created using many files imported in userCrome, if you do not want some features you can simply comment out lines with `@import` + transparency on popups is limited to urlbar, because you can't get blur on them and it looks bad without it + window transparencies make problems on non GNU/Linux systems, but this is not problem by default ## Installation 1. Open `about:config` and set this preferences to **'true'** + **`toolkit.legacyUserProfileCustomizations.stylesheets`** + **`svg.context-properties.content.enabled`** + **`layers.acceleration.force-enabled`** + **`gfx.webrender.all`** + **`gfx.webrender.enabled`** + **`layout.css.backdrop-filter.enabled`** 2. Go to `about:profiles` and find your `Root Directory` and enter there. 3. Next step you can solve with two ways: + create new directory `chrome` and copy there repo content + open terminal, when you are in `Root Directory` use `git clone https://github.com/Filip-Sutkowy/blurclean-firefox-theme chrome` ## Optional steps + Install TreeStyleTab addon and paste content of `tst_theme.css` in Tree Style Tab / Preferences / Advanced / Extra style. + Uncomment optional TST styles in userChrome + Uncomment optional transparencies for window (only for GNU/Linux) + move `Bookmarks toolbar items` next to url bar to save some vertical space ## Blur First, make sure that you have enabled optional styles in userChrome. Then you have to enable blur in compositor, where to find this option is based on your desktop environment, here is how to enable it with KDE: + Enable blur in System Settings / Workspace / Workspace Behavior / Deskop Effects + Download and enable **Force Blur** script in System Settings / Workspace / Window Management / KWin Scripts. Relogin to enable script configuration. Now add firefox in script config. + **Force Blur** need this two command to start working (maybe it's already fixed, check comments [here](https://store.kde.org/p/1294604/)) + `mkdir -p ~/.local/share/kservices5/` + `cp ~/.local/share/kwin/scripts/forceblur/metadata.desktop ~/.local/share/kservices5/forceblur.desktop` + Now should working ## Reccomended Addons These addons make this theme better + [TST Colored Tabs](https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/tst-colored-tabs) - You can use it instead of colouring in `tst_theme.css`, I don't use this addon because you can't get tranparency on tabs. + [Dark Reader](https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/darkreader) | [Dark Background and Light Text](https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/dark-background-light-text/) | [Darker Background Brighter Font](https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/darker-bg-brighter-font) or other addons which makes every page **DARK** + [Tabliss](https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/tabliss/) | [Momentum](https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/momentumdash) | [Speed Dial](https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/fvd-speed-dial) or other new page extension ## Uninstalling Just remove `chrome` folder, you can also reset changed config options, but this is not nessesary.

parts.text:
**Exploring extension configuration**

I’m considering using a third-party enterprise policy to configure the extension. I think I need to research the import/export settings for Sidebery, which might be useful. However, it could be simpler to stick to Firefox and Chrome only. I wonder if I should fetch information from the Sidebery wiki for custom styles or maybe look at GitHub for relevant resources. Let’s see what I can find!

content.summary:
(b3)

User later asked whether the first Firefox setup captured live `about:config` preferences. Assistant explained it only synced prefs explicitly listed in `scripts/setup/firefox/user.js`, not existing live `prefs.js` changes. User requested: “yes do thst we want our existiing config in place”. A read/write implementation followed: `scripts/setup/firefox-capture-prefs.py` was added as an executable Python helper that reads the Firefox default profile `prefs.js`, filters portable/safe `user_pref(...)` entries, and merges them into `scripts/setup/firefox/user.js` inside `// BEGIN captured Firefox preferences` / `// END captured Firefox preferences`. The helper parses `profiles.ini`, supports `--dry-run`, `--profile`, `--user-js`, `--firefox-root`, and `--self-test`, and excludes local/runtime-sensitive prefs such as `/Users/...`, `services.sync.*`, `identity.fxaccounts.*`, telemetry/client IDs, download paths, cache/update/session counters, extension runtime UUIDs, and token/secret/password-like values. `scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh` gained `CAPTURE_PREFS_HELPER`, a `capture_current_prefs()` function, and `--capture-current-prefs`. Running the helper on `/Users/shahe.islam/Library/Application Support/Firefox/Profiles/ewb7hoy2.default-release` ultimately captured 43 safe prefs into `scripts/setup/firefox/user.js`; the block includes portable UI/privacy/devtools prefs like contentblocking category, ctrlTab ordering, IP protection flag, bookmarks toolbar visibility, toolbar customization JSON, devtools prefs, HTTPS-only-ever-enabled, active theme ID, network prefetch/DNS prefs, PDF alt-text pref, semantic history prefs, Sidebery/sidebar prefs, and `signon.rememberSignons=false`. Verification showed `user.js` had no `/Users/`, `shahe`, `token`, `secret`, `password`, `credential`, `services.sync`, `identity.fxaccounts`, or `download.lastDir` matches. `.granted/README.md` was updated with `~/dotfiles/scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh --capture-current-prefs` instructions. `scripts/test-filter.sh` was updated to compile/self-test the capture helper and assert the captured block and local-path exclusion. Validation after this capture work passed: Python compile, capture helper self-test, `bash -n scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh`, `bash scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh --self-test`, ShellCheck when available, `scripts/test-filter.sh browser` 34/34, `scripts/test-filter.sh setup-syntax` 6/6, `scripts/test-filter.sh brewfile` 2/2, `scripts/smoke-test.sh` 30/30, and `git diff --check`.

(b5)

Current uncommitted Firefox work before the active aesthetic-planning request: modified `.granted/README.md`, `homebrew/Brewfile`, `scripts/setup.sh`, and `scripts/test-filter.sh`; untracked `scripts/setup/firefox-capture-prefs.py`, `scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh`, and `scripts/setup/firefox/` with `policies.json`, `user.js`, and `chrome/userChrome.css`. No commit was made.

parts.text: ▣ DCP | -383K removed, +34K summary — Compression #6

**Considering toolbar customization**

I'm thinking about how many extension buttons are on the navigation bar and the unified extension area. There might be some minimalism improvements to consider, like not hard-coding the toolbar state. The toolbar customization feature with extension IDs sounds interesting, but it might not work across devices and could lead to clutter. Although, I'm wondering if this is really relevant to the question, since it might not be about Firefox configuration specifically.

I found blurclean is mostly `userChrome.css`/`userContent.css` imports, with transparency/blur focused around the URL bar and popups. I’m checking Sidebery’s own style snippets too so the plan stays compatible with the sidebar workflow rather than blindly importing a full theme.

content.summary:
Read-only research for user question: “Are there any other Firefox config changes that you'd suggest for minimalism? https://github.com/Filip-Sutkowy/blurclean-firefox-theme Can we use something like this to improve the aesthetics of Firefox?” No files were edited. Plan Mode became active during this range, so subsequent work must remain read-only until user approves implementation.

Repo state read:
- Existing dotfiles-managed Firefox chrome CSS is `/Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/scripts/setup/firefox/chrome/userChrome.css`, currently 29 lines. It only hides Firefox native horizontal tabs for Sidebery using `@media not -moz-pref("sidebar.verticalTabs") { #TabsToolbar:not([customizing]) { visibility: collapse !important; } ... }` and preserves fullscreen/titlebar buttonbox/spacers with macOS handling.
- Existing dotfiles-managed Firefox prefs are `/Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/scripts/setup/firefox/user.js`. Important current prefs:
  - Containers enabled: `privacy.userContext.enabled=true`, `privacy.userContext.ui.enabled=true`, `privacy.userContext.longPressBehavior=2`.
  - userChrome enabled: `toolkit.legacyUserProfileCustomizations.stylesheets=true`.
  - Minimal/noise prefs: disables default-browser prompt, first-run override, default bookmarks restore, activity-stream telemetry/sponsored entries, studies/datareporting/telemetry/Pocket.
  - Captured safe prefs include `browser.toolbars.bookmarks.visibility="never"`, Sidebery/sidebar prefs such as `sidebar.visibility="hide-sidebar"`, `sidebar.revamp=true`, `sidebar.main.tools=...{3c078156-979c-498b-8990-85f7987dd929}`, toolbar customization JSON with many extension IDs, active Alpenglow theme, network prefetch disables, and `signon.rememberSignons=false`.
- Existing `/Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh` installs `policies.json`, default-profile `user.js`, and `chrome/userChrome.css`; has `USER_CHROME_SOURCE="$SCRIPT_DIR/firefox/chrome/userChrome.css"`; does not currently manage `userContent.css` or Sidebery internal styles. It supports `--capture-current-prefs`, `--self-test`; `install_user_chrome()` copies only `userChrome.css` into the default profile.
- Existing `/Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/scripts/setup/firefox/policies.json` force-installs Granted extension and sets container prefs via enterprise `Preferences`, but no aesthetic prefs.
- Existing `scripts/test-filter.sh test_browser()` checks Firefox cask/setup/helper, helper self-test, capture helper, policies, `user.js`, `userChrome.css`, and hidden tabs. It does not test `userContent.css`, sidebar-header minimization, blurclean-inspired rules, or Sidebery internal CSS.
- `.granted/README.md` already documents `userChrome.css` hiding native tabs, the full Firefox restart requirement, and troubleshooting for native top tabs still showing.

Blurclean repo findings from `https://github.com/Filip-Sutkowy/blurclean-firefox-theme`:
- It is a CSS-only Firefox theme with 112 stars, no releases shown, and GitHub license API returned 404, so no explicit repository license was found. Avoid vendoring the whole theme into dotfiles unless license is clarified; safer to use as design inspiration and write small local CSS.
- `README.md` says it supports dark/light themes, was tested on Manjaro KDE and Windows 10, and by default “only brings back icons in menus and makes urlbar transparent”. It says transparency on popups is limited to urlbar, and “window transparencies make problems on non GNU/Linux systems” unless optional window transparency is enabled.
- It requires these about:config prefs for full effect: `toolkit.legacyUserProfileCustomizations.stylesheets`, `svg.context-properties.content.enabled`, `layers.acceleration.force-enabled`, `gfx.webrender.all`, `gfx.webrender.enabled`, `layout.css.backdrop-filter.enabled`.
- Root `userChrome.css` imports `components/variables.css`, optional `window-control-placeholder.css`, optional `hide-tabbar.css`, `components/menu-icons.css`, `components/elements-transparencies.css`, optional `window-transparencies.css`, and optional `tst-tweaks.css`. For our setup, we already have a better Sidebery-compatible hide-tabs rule from MrOtherGuy; blurclean’s TST-specific files are not directly applicable because user uses Sidebery, not Tree Style Tab.
- `userContent.css` only imports variables and sets `about:blank` background to `var(--bct-w-pages-bg)` to avoid a white loading page.
- `components/variables.css` defines blurclean variables: `--bct-blur: 10px`, dark `--bct-bg: #00000088`, `--bct-color: #F2F2F2`, URL/popups/sidebar backgrounds, light/dark variants, and transparent-window variables.
- `components/elements-transparencies.css` makes `#widget-overflow-mainView` and open `#urlbar-background` use semi-transparent blurred backgrounds (`backdrop-filter: contrast(100%) blur(var(--bct-blur)) saturate(25%)`) and removes borders. It also blurs the customize-panel overflow menu.
- `components/menu-icons.css` is large (~17k) and adds icons to many menus/context menus. It may improve visual affordance but is not strictly minimal; it also has selector fragility across Firefox updates.
- `components/window-control-placeholder.css` reserves nav-bar space for window controls. On macOS it uses `@supports -moz-bool-pref("layout.css.osx-font-smoothing.enabled")` and `--uc-window-control-width: 72px`. Our current CSS already handles macOS titlebar spacers; only copy specific placeholder behavior if a top-left window-control overlap appears.
- `components/hide-tabbar.css` is older MrOtherGuy code and notes OSX should use `hide_tabs_toolbar_osx.css`; our current `hide_tabs_toolbar_v2`-inspired CSS is preferable.

Sidebery official wiki findings:
- Raw wiki page `Firefox-Styles-Snippets-(via-userChrome.css).md` confirms custom Firefox chrome CSS may break after Firefox updates and requires `toolkit.legacyUserProfileCustomizations.stylesheets=true`.
- For “Completely hide native tabs strip”, Sidebery recommends MrOtherGuy `hide_tabs_toolbar`, `hide_tabs_toolbar_osx`, and `window_control_placeholder_support`. This matches the existing implementation direction.
- Sidebery provides a “Decrease size of the sidebar header” snippet:
  - `#sidebar-header { font-size: 1.2em !important; padding: 2px 6px 2px 3px !important; }`
  - `#sidebar-header #sidebar-close { padding: 3px !important; }`
  - `#sidebar-header #sidebar-close .toolbarbutton-icon { width: 14px !important; height: 14px !important; opacity: 0.6 !important; }`
- Sidebery wiki also lists a GNOME-theme variant where `#sidebar-header { display: none; }`, and notes setting `sidebar.revamp=false` can hide the native sidebar revamp/tools area if desired.
- Raw `Sidebery-Styles-Snippets.md` says Sidebery’s own internal styles are edited in Sidebery settings > Styles editor or Appearance > Edit styles. It has selectors for tabs and nav bar. It provides examples for favicons on the right, multiline titles, and vertical panel labels. For our dotfiles setup, Sidebery internal CSS is harder to sync automatically unless we manage extension storage or provide a manual snippet; userChrome CSS can style Firefox chrome around Sidebery but not necessarily all Sidebery internals.

Recommended minimalism/aesthetic conclusions:
- Do not import blurclean wholesale. It is unlicensed in GitHub metadata, older/small, not tested on macOS, includes non-minimal menu-icon bulk, and optional window transparency is explicitly problematic on non-GNU/Linux systems.
- Use a small blurclean-inspired local layer instead: keep existing hidden native tab strip; reduce or optionally hide the Firefox sidebar header; add minimal translucent/blurred URL bar and popup backgrounds; add `about:blank`/new-tab blank background via `userContent.css` to avoid white flashes; optionally set `svg.context-properties.content.enabled` and `layout.css.backdrop-filter.enabled` in `user.js`; avoid forcing `gfx.webrender.*`/`layers.acceleration.force-enabled` unless the CSS visibly needs it, because modern Firefox usually has WebRender/acceleration already and forced graphics prefs are less minimal.
- Suggested minimal configs if implementing later:
  - Extend `userChrome.css` with theme variables matching dotfiles/Tokyo Night, e.g. `--df-firefox-bg: rgba(26,27,38,0.78)`, `--df-firefox-border: rgba(122,162,247,0.20)`, `--df-firefox-radius: 10px`, `--df-firefox-blur: 14px`.
  - Add URL bar focus/open styling: transparent or Tokyo Night surface, rounded, subtle border, backdrop blur only when supported.
  - Add PanelUI/urlbar result popup styling with the same background/border/radius, but keep rules small.
  - Reduce `#sidebar-header` first rather than hiding it; hiding can make sidebar switching/closing less discoverable. Hide it only if user wants maximum vertical minimalism.
  - Consider `sidebar.revamp=false` if user wants fewer native Firefox sidebar/tool controls, but this may interact with captured `sidebar.main.tools`/Sidebery usage and should be tested visually.
  - Add `scripts/setup/firefox/chrome/userContent.css` for `about:blank` and maybe `about:newtab` background only, then update `firefox-setup.sh` with `USER_CONTENT_SOURCE` and `install_user_content()` copying to `$profile_path/chrome/userContent.css`.
  - Update `scripts/test-filter.sh` browser tests for `userContent.css`, `install_user_content`, blur variables, sidebar-header minimization, and required prefs.
  - Update `.granted/README.md` to document minimal Firefox theme layer and restart requirement.
  - Apply locally by running `bash scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh` and restarting Firefox, but only after leaving Plan Mode.
- Potential risks/tradeoffs:
  - Firefox chrome selectors are unstable and may break on future Firefox releases.
  - `backdrop-filter` can be expensive; use it only for small surfaces like URL bar/panels, not whole window.
  - Full window transparency/blur is not recommended on macOS based on blurclean docs.
  - Managing Sidebery internal styles automatically is a separate task; userChrome can reduce the Firefox sidebar header, but Sidebery tab styling itself likely needs Sidebery’s own Styles editor or extension-storage management.
  - Current captured `browser.uiCustomization.state` and `browser.uiCustomization.horizontalTabsBackup` hardcode extension IDs and a device-specific toolbar layout. For minimalism, consider replacing them with a deliberately lean toolbar layout or excluding them from future pref capture, but this is a bigger behavior choice because user asked to preserve existing config.

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: #!/usr/bin/env bash
2: # Apply dotfiles-managed Firefox policy and default-profile preferences on macOS.
3: 
4: set -euo pipefail
5: 
6: SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
7: FIREFOX_APP="${FIREFOX_APP:-/Applications/Firefox.app}"
8: FIREFOX_ROOT="${FIREFOX_ROOT:-$HOME/Library/Application Support/Firefox}"
9: POLICY_SOURCE="${FIREFOX_POLICY_SOURCE:-$SCRIPT_DIR/firefox/policies.json}"
10: USER_JS_SOURCE="${FIREFOX_USER_JS_SOURCE:-$SCRIPT_DIR/firefox/user.js}"
11: USER_CHROME_SOURCE="${FIREFOX_USER_CHROME_SOURCE:-$SCRIPT_DIR/firefox/chrome/userChrome.css}"
12: CAPTURE_PREFS_HELPER="$SCRIPT_DIR/firefox-capture-prefs.py"
13: 
14: BLUE='\033[0;34m'
15: GREEN='\033[0;32m'
16: YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
17: NC='\033[0m'
18: 
19: log_info() {
20:     echo -e "${BLUE}[INFO]${NC} $1"
21: }
22: 
23: log_success() {
24:     echo -e "${GREEN}[SUCCESS]${NC} $1"
25: }
26: 
27: log_warning() {
28:     echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARNING]${NC} $1"
29: }
30: 
31: backup_existing_file() {
32:     local target_path="$1"
33:     local backup_path=""
34: 
35:     backup_path="${target_path}.backup.$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)"
36: 
37:     if cp "$target_path" "$backup_path"; then
38:         log_info "Backed up existing $(basename "$target_path") to $backup_path"
39:         return 0
40:     fi
41: 
42:     log_warning "Could not back up existing $(basename "$target_path")"
43:     return 1
44: }
45: 
46: copy_managed_file() {
47:     local source_path="$1"
48:     local target_path="$2"
49:     local label="$3"
50: 
51:     if [[ ! -f "$source_path" ]]; then
52:         log_warning "$label source missing: $source_path"
53:         return 0
54:     fi
55: 
56:     if [[ -f "$target_path" ]] && cmp -s "$source_path" "$target_path"; then
57:         log_success "$label already current"
58:         return 0
59:     fi
60: 
61:     if [[ -e "$target_path" && ! -f "$target_path" && ! -L "$target_path" ]]; then
62:         log_warning "$label target exists but is not a file: $target_path"
63:         return 1
64:     fi
65: 
66:     if ! mkdir -p "$(dirname "$target_path")" 2>/dev/null; then
67:         log_warning "Could not create $(dirname "$target_path")"
68:         return 1
69:     fi
70: 
71:     if [[ -f "$target_path" || -L "$target_path" ]]; then
72:         if ! backup_existing_file "$target_path"; then
73:             return 1
74:         fi
75: 
76:         if ! rm -f "$target_path"; then
77:             log_warning "Could not replace existing $label at $target_path"
78:             return 1
79:         fi
80:     fi
81: 
82:     if cp "$source_path" "$target_path"; then
83:         chmod 0644 "$target_path" 2>/dev/null || true
84:         log_success "Installed $label"
85:         return 0
86:     fi
87: 
88:     log_warning "Could not install $label to $target_path"
89:     return 1
90: }
91: 
92: resolve_profile_path() {
93:     local profile_ref="$1"
94: 
95:     if [[ "$profile_ref" == /* ]]; then
96:         printf '%s\n' "$profile_ref"
97:     else
98:         printf '%s/%s\n' "$FIREFOX_ROOT" "$profile_ref"
99:     fi
100: }
101: 
102: find_default_profile() {
103:     local profiles_ini="$FIREFOX_ROOT/profiles.ini"
104:     local section=""
105:     local path=""
106:     local is_relative="1"
107:     local is_default="0"
108:     local install_default=""
109:     local profile_default=""
110:     local profile_default_relative="1"
111:     local first_profile=""
112:     local first_profile_relative="1"
113: 
114:     [[ -f "$profiles_ini" ]] || return 1
115: 
116:     commit_profile_section() {
117:         if [[ "$section" != Profile* || -z "$path" ]]; then
118:             return 0
119:         fi
120: 
121:         if [[ -z "$first_profile" ]]; then
122:             first_profile="$path"
123:             first_profile_relative="$is_relative"
124:         fi
125: 
126:         if [[ "$is_default" == "1" ]]; then
127:             profile_default="$path"
128:             profile_default_relative="$is_relative"
129:         fi
130:     }
131: 
132:     while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
133:         line="${line%$'\r'}"
134: 
135:         case "$line" in
136:         '' | \#* | \;*)
137:             continue
138:             ;;
139:         \[*\])
140:             commit_profile_section
141:             section="${line#[}"
142:             section="${section%]}"
143:             path=""
144:             is_relative="1"
145:             is_default="0"
146:             continue
147:             ;;
148:         esac
149: 
150:         case "$section" in
151:         Install*)
152:             case "$line" in
153:             Default=*) install_default="${line#Default=}" ;;
154:             esac
155:             ;;
156:         Profile*)
157:             case "$line" in
158:             Path=*) path="${line#Path=}" ;;
159:             IsRelative=*) is_relative="${line#IsRelative=}" ;;
160:             Default=1) is_default="1" ;;
161:             esac
162:             ;;
163:         esac
164:     done <"$profiles_ini"
165: 
166:     commit_profile_section
167: 
168:     if [[ -n "$install_default" ]]; then
169:         resolve_profile_path "$install_default"
170:         return 0
171:     fi
172: 
173:     if [[ -n "$profile_default" ]]; then
174:         if [[ "$profile_default_relative" == "1" ]]; then
175:             resolve_profile_path "$profile_default"
176:         else
177:             printf '%s\n' "$profile_default"
178:         fi
179:         return 0
180:     fi
181: 
182:     if [[ -n "$first_profile" ]]; then
183:         if [[ "$first_profile_relative" == "1" ]]; then
184:             resolve_profile_path "$first_profile"
185:         else
186:             printf '%s\n' "$first_profile"
187:         fi
188:         return 0
189:     fi
190: 
191:     return 1
192: }
193: 
194: install_policy() {
195:     local policy_target="$FIREFOX_APP/Contents/Resources/distribution/policies.json"
196: 
197:     if [[ ! -d "$FIREFOX_APP" ]]; then
198:         log_warning "Firefox.app not found at $FIREFOX_APP; skipping enterprise policy"
199:         return 0
200:     fi
201: 
202:     if copy_managed_file "$POLICY_SOURCE" "$policy_target" "Firefox policies.json"; then
203:         return 0
204:     fi
205: 
206:     log_warning "Manual policy install: sudo mkdir -p \"$(dirname "$policy_target")\" && sudo cp \"$POLICY_SOURCE\" \"$policy_target\""
207:     return 1
208: }
209: 
210: install_user_js() {
211:     local profile_path=""
212: 
213:     if ! profile_path="$(find_default_profile)"; then
214:         log_warning "No Firefox profile found; run Firefox once, then rerun setup to install user.js"
215:         return 0
216:     fi
217: 
218:     if [[ ! -d "$profile_path" ]]; then
219:         log_warning "Default Firefox profile directory does not exist: $profile_path"
220:         return 0
221:     fi
222: 
223:     copy_managed_file "$USER_JS_SOURCE" "$profile_path/user.js" "Firefox default-profile user.js" || true
224: }
225: 
226: install_user_chrome() {
227:     local profile_path=""
228: 
229:     if ! profile_path="$(find_default_profile)"; then
230:         log_warning "No Firefox profile found; run Firefox once, then rerun setup to install userChrome.css"
231:         return 0
232:     fi
233: 
234:     if [[ ! -d "$profile_path" ]]; then
235:         log_warning "Default Firefox profile directory does not exist: $profile_path"
236:         return 0
237:     fi
238: 
239:     copy_managed_file "$USER_CHROME_SOURCE" "$profile_path/chrome/userChrome.css" "Firefox Sidebery userChrome.css" || true
240: }
241: 
242: capture_current_prefs() {
243:     if ! command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
244:         log_warning "python3 is required to capture Firefox prefs"
245:         return 1
246:     fi
247: 
248:     python3 "$CAPTURE_PREFS_HELPER" --firefox-root "$FIREFOX_ROOT" --user-js "$USER_JS_SOURCE" "$@"
249: }
250: 
251: self_test() {
252:     local tmp_dir=""
253:     local profile_dir=""
254:     local old_firefox_app=""
255:     local old_firefox_root=""
256:     local result=0
257: 
258:     old_firefox_app="$FIREFOX_APP"
259:     old_firefox_root="$FIREFOX_ROOT"
260: 
261:     tmp_dir="$(mktemp -d)"
262:     profile_dir="$tmp_dir/Firefox/Profiles/test.default"
263: 
264:     mkdir -p "$profile_dir" "$tmp_dir/Firefox.app"
265:     printf '%s\n' \
266:         '[Install1234567890]' \
267:         'Default=Profiles/test.default' \
268:         '[Profile0]' \
269:         'Name=default' \
270:         'IsRelative=1' \
271:         'Path=Profiles/test.default' \
272:         'Default=1' >"$tmp_dir/Firefox/profiles.ini"
273: 
274:     FIREFOX_APP="$tmp_dir/Firefox.app"
275:     FIREFOX_ROOT="$tmp_dir/Firefox"
276: 
277:     install_policy >/dev/null
278:     install_user_js >/dev/null
279:     install_user_chrome >/dev/null
280:     install_policy >/dev/null
281:     install_user_js >/dev/null
282:     install_user_chrome >/dev/null
283: 
284:     [[ -f "$FIREFOX_APP/Contents/Resources/distribution/policies.json" ]] || result=1
285:     [[ -f "$profile_dir/user.js" ]] || result=1
286:     [[ -f "$profile_dir/chrome/userChrome.css" ]] || result=1
287:     compgen -G "$profile_dir/user.js.backup.*" >/dev/null && result=1
288:     compgen -G "$profile_dir/chrome/userChrome.css.backup.*" >/dev/null && result=1
289: 
290:     FIREFOX_APP="$old_firefox_app"
291:     FIREFOX_ROOT="$old_firefox_root"
292:     rm -rf "$tmp_dir"
293: 
294:     return "$result"
295: }
296: 
297: main() {
298:     case "${1:-}" in
299:     --self-test)
300:         self_test
301:         return 0
302:         ;;
303:     --capture-current-prefs)
304:         shift
305:         capture_current_prefs "$@"

309:

311:         log_info "Firefox setup is macOS-only; skipping"

313:     fi

315:     if command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then

# Apply dotfiles-managed Firefox policy and default-profile preferences on macOS.

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
FIREFOX_APP="${FIREFOX_APP:-/Applications/Firefox.app}"

POLICY_SOURCE="${FIREFOX_POLICY_SOURCE:-$SCRIPT_DIR/firefox/policies.json}"
USER_JS_SOURCE="${FIREFOX_USER_JS_SOURCE:-$SCRIPT_DIR/firefox/user.js}"
USER_CHROME_SOURCE="${FIREFOX_USER_CHROME_SOURCE:-$SCRIPT_DIR/firefox/chrome/userChrome.css}"
CAPTURE_PREFS_HELPER="$SCRIPT_DIR/firefox-capture-prefs.py"

BLUE='\033[0;34m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
NC='\033[0m'

log_info() {
    echo -e "${BLUE}[INFO]${NC} $1"

# Scripts Index

112 scripts and 20 subdirectories supporting the dotfiles ecosystem. Scripts use `#!/usr/bin/env bash` with `set -euo pipefail`.

## Key Entry Points

| Script | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `setup.sh` | Main machine bootstrap (Homebrew, stow, tools, configs) |

| `setup-selfhost-llm.sh` | Ollama + Open WebUI local LLM |

## Subdirectories

### Core Infrastructure
| Directory | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| `setup/` | macOS defaults, system preferences |
| `harness/` | Drift detection, diagnostics for dotfiles health |
| `lib/` | Shared bash libraries and utilities |
| `bin/` | Standalone executables added to PATH |
| `hooks/` | Git hooks and CI integration |
| `ci-hooks/` | CI-specific hook scripts |
| `tests/` | Shell script tests |
| `profiles/` | Shell profile management |

### Agent & AI

|-----------|---------|

### DevOps & Containers
| Directory | Purpose |

| Directory | Purpose |

| `tmux/` | tmux watcher, session management |
| `pihole/` | Pi-hole DNS management |
| `ticket-queue/` | Ticket queue management |
| `tools/` | Standalone tool scripts |

## Conventions

- Bash scripts: `set -euo pipefail`, quote all variables, snake_case functions
- File naming: kebab-case (e.g., `setup-mobile-coding.sh`)
- Shared code: import from `lib/` directory
- Never use `((var++))` with `set -e` — use `var=$((var + 1))` instead

        run_test "setup.sh registers .mcp.json stdio servers" "python3 -c \"import json, pathlib, re; servers=set(json.load(open('$mcp_config')).get('mcpServers', {})); setup=pathlib.Path('$DOTFILES_ROOT/scripts/setup.sh').read_text(); configured=set(re.findall(r'claude mcp add --scope user ([a-z0-9-]+) ', setup)); assert servers <= configured\""
    fi

    run_test "setup.sh uses bunx for shared stdio MCP servers" "python3 -c \"import pathlib; text=pathlib.Path('$DOTFILES_ROOT/scripts/setup.sh').read_text(); required=['context7 bunx', 'steampipe bunx', 'playwright bunx', 'drawio bunx']; assert all(item in text for item in required)\""
    run_test "setup.sh keeps deepwiki SSE exception" "grep -q 'claude mcp add --scope user --transport sse deepwiki https://mcp.deepwiki.com/sse' '$DOTFILES_ROOT/scripts/setup.sh'"

    # deniedMcpServers: blocks unused claude.ai managed integrations to reclaim KV-cache tokens
    local settings="$DOTFILES_ROOT/.claude/settings.json"
    if [ -f "$settings" ]; then
        run_test "settings.json declares deniedMcpServers" "python3 -c \"import json; s=json.load(open('$settings')); assert isinstance(s.get('deniedMcpServers'), list) and len(s['deniedMcpServers']) > 0\""
        run_test "deniedMcpServers includes unused integrations" "python3 -c \"import json; denied=set(json.load(open('$settings')).get('deniedMcpServers', [])); required={'claude_ai_Notion','claude_ai_Linear','claude_ai_Invideo'}; assert required <= denied, f'missing: {required - denied}'\""
        run_test "deniedMcpServers keeps used integrations unblocked" "python3 -c \"import json; denied=set(json.load(open('$settings')).get('deniedMcpServers', [])); used={'claude_ai_Atlassian','claude_ai_Gmail','claude_ai_Google_Calendar','claude_ai_Google_Drive'}; assert not (used & denied), f'blocks used: {used & denied}'\""

<path>/Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.granted/README.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # Granted Configuration Guide
2: 
3: This directory contains configuration for [Granted](https://granted.dev), an AWS credential management tool with autocomplete, browser integration, and Firefox container colors/icons.
4: 
5: ## What You Should See
6: 
7: ### Terminal Autocomplete
8: 
9: When you type `assume` followed by space and Tab:
10: 
11: ```bash
12: assume <TAB>
13: ```
14: 
15: Expected: AWS profile names from `~/.aws/config`.
16: 
17: Not expected: Granted command options or local file names.
18: 
19: ### Firefox Browser Integration
20: 
21: When you open an AWS console through Granted:
22: 
23: - Firefox opens instead of Chrome or Safari.
24: - Granted opens the console in a Firefox container tab.
25: - Container colors/icons come from `.granted/firefox-profiles`.
26: 
27: Examples:
28: 
29: ```text
30: production=red:briefcase
31: development=green:tree
32: staging=yellow:cart
33: security=purple:fingerprint
34: ```
35: 
36: ## Setup
37: 
38: ### Automatic Setup
39: 
40: The dotfiles setup script handles the reproducible pieces:
41: 
42: ```bash
43: ./scripts/setup.sh
44: ```
45: 
46: Setup does the following:
47: 
48: - Installs Firefox from `homebrew/Brewfile` and `phase_9_fonts_and_apps`.
49: - Uses stow to sync `.granted/config` and `.granted/firefox-profiles` into `~/.granted`.
50: - Applies `scripts/setup/firefox/policies.json` to the Firefox app bundle when writable.
51: - Installs `scripts/setup/firefox/user.js` into the default local Firefox profile only.
52: - Installs `scripts/setup/firefox/chrome/userChrome.css` into the default local profile to hide native horizontal tabs when using Sidebery.
53: 
54: The `user.js` syncs selected `about:config` preferences without taking ownership of cookies, history, sessions, logins, cache, or the full Firefox profile.
55: 
56: To refresh `user.js` from the current Firefox default profile:
57: 
58: ```bash
59: ~/dotfiles/scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh --capture-current-prefs
60: ```
61: 
62: The capture helper reads `prefs.js`, keeps a conservative allowlist of portable preferences, and excludes profile/session/device-specific values such as local paths, Sync account state, telemetry IDs, cache state, downloads, and extension runtime UUIDs.
63: 
64: ### Manual Fallback
65: 
66: If automatic Firefox policy installation is skipped because the app bundle is not writable:
67: 
68: ```bash
69: sudo mkdir -p "/Applications/Firefox.app/Contents/Resources/distribution"
70: sudo cp ~/dotfiles/scripts/setup/firefox/policies.json "/Applications/Firefox.app/Contents/Resources/distribution/policies.json"
71: ```
72: 
73: If Firefox has not created a profile yet, launch Firefox once and then rerun:
74: 
75: ```bash
76: ~/dotfiles/scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh
77: ```
78: 
79: If the Granted extension is still missing, install it from AMO:
80: 
81: ```text
82: https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/granted/
83: ```
84: 
85: Check active Firefox enterprise policies at `about:policies`.
86: 
87: ## Configuration Files
88: 
89: ### `.granted/config`
90: 
91: Main Granted configuration:
92: 
93: - Sets Firefox as the default Granted browser.
94: - Points Granted at `/Applications/Firefox.app/Contents/MacOS/firefox`.
95: - Uses the macOS keychain backend.
96: 
97: ### `.granted/firefox-profiles`
98: 
99: Maps AWS profile names to Firefox container colors and icons:
100: 
101: ```text
102: production=red:briefcase
103: prod=red:briefcase
104: development=green:tree
105: dev=green:tree
106: management=blue:briefcase
107: security=purple:fingerprint
108: ```
109: 
110: ### `scripts/setup/firefox/policies.json`
111: 
112: Machine-level Firefox enterprise policies:
113: 
114: - Force-installs the Granted Firefox extension.
115: - Disables Firefox studies, telemetry, Pocket, default-browser prompts, and first-run/post-update pages.
116: 
117: ### `scripts/setup/firefox/user.js`
118: 
119: Default-profile `about:config` preferences:
120: 
121: - Enables Firefox containers and the container UI.
122: - Mirrors lightweight telemetry/new-tab defaults.
123: - Includes a generated block of safe preferences captured from the current local Firefox default profile.
124: - Does not manage profile databases such as `cookies.sqlite`, `places.sqlite`, `logins.json`, `key4.db`, or session restore files.
125: 
126: ### `scripts/setup/firefox/chrome/userChrome.css`
127: 
128: Default-profile Firefox chrome CSS:
129: 
130: - Hides Firefox's native horizontal tab strip so Sidebery is the only tab UI.
131: - Keeps macOS/window-control support from the upstream Firefox CSS hack.
132: - Requires `toolkit.legacyUserProfileCustomizations.stylesheets=true`, which is set in `user.js`.
133: - Requires a full Firefox restart after installation.
134: 
135: ## Available Colors And Icons
136: 
137: Colors: blue, turquoise, green, yellow, orange, red, pink, purple.
138: 
139: Icons: fingerprint, briefcase, dollar, cart, circle, gift, vacation, food, fruit, pet, tree, chill.
140: 
141: ## Usage Examples
142: 
143: ```bash
144: # Basic profile switching
145: assume production
146: 
147: # Open AWS console in Firefox with the configured container
148: assume -c production
149: 
150: # List available profiles
151: granted profiles list
152: 
153: # Configure profile colors in the local Granted config
154: ~/dotfiles/scripts/setup/granted-setup.sh set-profile-color myprofile blue tree
155: 
156: # Test local Granted configuration
157: ~/dotfiles/scripts/setup/granted-setup.sh test-config
158: ```
159: 
160: ## Troubleshooting
161: 
162: ### Autocomplete Shows Wrong Items
163: 
164: Problem: `assume <TAB>` shows files or Granted commands instead of AWS profiles.
165: 
166: Solution:
167: 
168: ```bash
169: fish -c "source ~/.config/fish/completions/granted.fish"
170: ```
171: 
172: ### No Colors Or Icons In Firefox
173: 
174: Problem: Firefox tabs are plain, with no Granted colors or icons.
175: 
176: Solution:
177: 
178: 1. Check `about:policies` for the Granted extension policy.
179: 2. Confirm the Granted Firefox addon is installed: `about:addons`.
180: 3. Ensure Firefox is set as Granted's browser: `granted browser set firefox`.
181: 4. Check profile mappings in `~/.granted/firefox-profiles`.
182: 
183: ### About:Config Settings Did Not Apply
184: 
185: Problem: Firefox preferences from dotfiles are missing.
186: 
187: Solution:
188: 
189: 1. Quit Firefox completely.
190: 2. Run `~/dotfiles/scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh`.
191: 3. Reopen Firefox.
192: 4. Check that the default profile has `user.js` under `~/Library/Application Support/Firefox/Profiles/<profile>/user.js`.
193: 
194: ### Native Top Tabs Still Show With Sidebery
195: 
196: Problem: Sidebery is open, but Firefox still shows the native horizontal tab bar at the top.
197: 
198: Solution:
199: 
200: 1. Quit Firefox completely.
201: 2. Run `~/dotfiles/scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh`.
202: 3. Reopen Firefox.
203: 4. Check that the default profile has `chrome/userChrome.css` under `~/Library/Application Support/Firefox/Profiles/<profile>/chrome/userChrome.css`.
204: 5. Confirm `toolkit.legacyUserProfileCustomizations.stylesheets` is `true` in `about:config`.
205: 
206: ### Wrong Browser Opens
207: 
208: Problem: Chrome or Safari opens instead of Firefox.
209: 
210: Solution:
211: 
212: ```bash
213: granted browser set firefox
214: ```
215: 
216: ### Profile Not Found
217: 
218: Problem: Granted reports profile-not-found errors.
219: 
220: Solution:
221: 
222: 1. Check AWS configuration: `aws configure list-profiles`.
223: 2. Verify AWS config exists: `ls ~/.aws/config`.
224: 3. Ensure profile names match `.granted/firefox-profiles` exactly.
225: 
226: ## Helper Scripts
227: 
228: Use `~/dotfiles/scripts/setup/granted-setup.sh` for Granted config management:
229: 
230: - `setup` installs local Granted config files by copying them from dotfiles.
231: - `set-profile-color PROFILE COLOR ICON` configures colors/icons.
232: - `list-profiles` shows configured Firefox profile mappings.
233: - `test-config` validates local Granted setup.
234: - `list-colors` shows available options.
235: 
236: Use `~/dotfiles/scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh` for Firefox policy, `user.js`, and Sidebery `userChrome.css` setup. Use `~/dotfiles/scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh --capture-current-prefs` to refresh the dotfiles-managed `user.js` from the current Firefox default profile.
237: 
238: ## Links
239: 
240: - Documentation: https://docs.commonfate.io/granted/
241: - Firefox Addon: https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/granted/
242: - GitHub: https://github.com/common-fate/granted

(End of file - total 242 lines)
</content>

 - Installs Firefox from `homebrew/Brewfile` and `phase_9_fonts_and_apps`.

+- Installs `scripts/setup/firefox/chrome/userContent.css` into the default local profile to keep blank/internal pages dark.
 
 The `user.js` syncs selected `about:config` preferences without taking ownership of cookies, history, sessions, logins, cache, or the full Firefox profile.
 
 To refresh `user.js` from the current Firefox default profile:
@@ -127,12 +128,22 @@
 
 Default-profile Firefox chrome CSS:
 
 - Hides Firefox's native horizontal tab strip so Sidebery is the only tab UI.

 - Keeps macOS/window-control support from the upstream Firefox CSS hack.

+
